title: P0: goods-factory coordinator nil-pointer PANICS THE DAEMON when claiming ships - fleet-wide outage, 3 panics
description: 2026-07-07 13:29:29: daemon DEAD (socket deadline-exceeded, no process). Panic: 'invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference' at navigation.(*Ship).AssignToContainer (ship.go:444) called from run_factory_coordinator.go:655 claimShipForFactory <- runNodeWorker <- executeLevelParallel(:607). Trigger: goods_factory-FAB_MATS-06e30318 (vmrj build) starting parallel workers 'BUY IRON using TORWIND-3' + 'BUY QUARTZ_SAND using TORWIND-6' - ships likely coordinator-owned/nil-assignment at claim -> nil deref. THREE panic entries in log (repeat on restart-recovery re-instantiation = crashloop until factory container is not recovered or code fixed). Impact: TOTAL fleet outage - all coordinators, contracts, mfg, fill dead; income zero. NOTE FOR RELAUNCH: the factory container may be re-adopted by restart recovery and re-panic - deploy the fix FIRST or scrub the factory container record before daemon start. vmrj's wait-loop fixed the launch-crash but the claim path dereferences a nil (probably Ship.assignment or the container ref when the ship is mid-coordinator-task).
close_reason: merged feace58, DEPLOYED (daemon pid 56599, build-stamp confirms live=feace58a0c8b, 0 panics since restart, coordinators healthy). ROOT CAUSE: nil CLOCK, not nil ship. The factory constructor was the LONE coordinator missing the 'if clock==nil { clock=NewRealClock() }' guard that every sibling has (parallel_manufacturing:123, scouting, scout_markets); main.go:449 passes nil expecting substitution -> h.clock stayed nil -> clock.Now() at ship.go:444 SIGSEGV'd. Stack smoking gun: AssignToContainer 3rd arg {0x0,0x0} nil-interface, ship non-nil. Surfaced by vmrj: its wait-loop (intact) let the factory REACH the claim path that was masked behind the launch-crash. FIX: (1) constructor substitutes RealClock (root, matches siblings, also covers ProductionExecutor/ForceRelease/Sleep); (2) claim path re-validates !ship.IsIdle() and SKIPS stale/coordinator-owned ships (no clobber); (3) defense-in-depth nil guards — a worker returns a clean node error (never nil,nil which would re-panic executeLevelParallel), so a bad claim degrades to a relaunchable factory failure, NEVER a daemon SIGSEGV. 4 TDD tests incl. the exact nil-clock RED reproducer + happy-path no-regression. EMERGENCY CONTAINMENT earlier: scrubbed 2 RUNNING goods_factory containers to FAILED (broke the crashloop); daemon stayed up throughout after that.

title: REGRESSION (sk68 D2): past next_wake_at is level-triggered — supervisor fires a wake EVERY TICK until captain re-declares; burned the 8/h cap
description: Live incident 2026-07-07 06:43-06:47 (Admiral-observed: 'heartbeat every few seconds'). After sk68 deploy (c4b90dc), a declared next_wake_at that passes without being re-declared becomes a STANDING wake condition: every 30s tick evaluates now>=next_wake_at -> wake, again and again — 8 wakes in ~4 min until max_sessions_per_hour capped it, then 'session cap reached' spam every tick. Root cause: D2 removed the suppression of overdue alarms but nothing CONSUMES the alarm after it fires. FIX (one-shot semantics): in evaluateWakeGate, the declared next_wake_at triggers only while UNSERVICED — fire iff next_wake_at > last_session (a wake after the alarm services it); once last_session >= next_wake_at, fall back to heartbeat cadence (last_session + HeartbeatMinutes). Test matrix: alarm future -> no wake; alarm past + last_session < alarm -> wake ONCE; after that wake (last_session >= alarm) -> NO further alarm wakes, heartbeat resumes; overdue-heartbeat protection (D2's point) still holds when NO alarm is declared. Also: rate-limit the 'session cap reached' log line (once per cap window, not per tick). MITIGATED live at 06:5x by emergency re-declare (+45m, thresholds restored to 3.4M/500k — captain had briefly had below=800k/no-above; inform captain). This is MY regression from tonight's sk68 merge; same Admiral order covers the repair.
close_reason: merged a758735, DEPLOYED: captain rebuilt, supervisor kickstarted pid 9662, VERIFIED QUIET (0 log lines across 2+ ticks; no spurious wakes). Root fix: next_wake_at is a ONE-SHOT alarm — governs the gate only while unserviced (*NextWakeAt > LastSession); once a session runs at/after the alarm it is spent and heartbeat cadence resumes (D2 anchor semantics preserved: anchor = later(LastSession, DeclaredAt); no-alarm overdue heartbeat still wakes). TestWakeGateNextWakeAtIsOneShot RED-proven against the regression; cap log rate-limited (TestCapReachedLogIsRateLimitedAcrossConsecutiveTicks). Incident timeline: detected 06:47 (Admiral report), mitigated 06:50 (emergency re-declare), root-fixed+deployed+verified by 07:0x. Damage: 8 max-effort captain turns (hourly cap contained it). Post-deploy: captain re-declared its own policy (next wake 07:20) — full loop healthy.

title: Construction pipeline terminalizes on one leg's permanent-fail - execution-layer all-or-nothing (planning-layer fixed in r900)
description: 2026-07-07 11:10:48: task a7233fae (FAB_MATS) hit 'no source to acquire from' during F45's below-floor dip, permanently failed after 3 retries -> 'Pipeline f0187282 failed: construction_delivery_failed' - whole pipeline terminal, healthy circuitry leg killed with it, fill frozen 1h+ unnoticed. Fix: (a) unsourceable-at-dispatch should BE the pending-supply state (park + re-check on supply recovery), not retry-to-permanent-death; (b) a permanently-failed task parks ITS leg only - sibling legs keep flowing. P1 because every supply dip re-kills the fill until this lands (captain back to heartbeat pokes as safety net). Full lineage in sp-r900 notes (CLOSED - this is its execution-layer successor).
close_reason: merged b93a041, DEPLOYED (daemon pid 49738, batched w/ 6fsq+vz9u+kb61; build-stamp CONFIRMS live binary = b93a04168907). LIVE PROOF: construction X1-KA42-I53 now at 31.5%, BOTH legs advancing independently — FAB_MATS 283/1600, ADV_CIRCUITRY 347/400 (86.8%); the all-or-nothing freeze is gone. Two-part fix: (a) 'no source to acquire' at execution -> ErrDeferToSupply sentinel -> worker parks (ParkForResupply: EXECUTING->PENDING, clears assignedShip, NO retryCount++) -> stays IsDeferredConstruction -> r900's SupplyMonitor re-sources verbatim when supply recovers (zero new recovery code); (b) evaluateConstructionCompletion reordered so inFlight>0 (incl. a parked leg) keeps the pipeline ALIVE, completes on any delivered material once all legs terminal, fails ONLY when nothing delivered AND every leg dead. b1np's all-dead test still green. 6 TDD tests across domain/executor/worker/checker/supplymonitor, RED-proven. RESIDUAL (filed follow-up): shape #2 'source exists but market dry' still retry->leg-stall per b1np's contract — but pipeline SURVIVES + siblings flow (captain-recoverable), NOT the old whole-pipeline freeze.

title: Watchkeeper auto-spawns captain + surveyor sessions, violating the no-auto-spawn policy (zombie-captain is a symptom)
description: SpawnSession (gobot/internal/captain/gc.go:91-100) runs session-new (l.92) -> acd prime (l.95) -> session submit (l.99). If session-new succeeds but prime or submit fails, it returns error while the session ALREADY EXISTS — live but never primed. ensureCaptainAlive (respawn.go:46) then sees SessionAlive=true and returns early forever; the supervisor believes the captain is alive while it sits inert. Compounding: the Admiral spawn-error alert is '_ ='-swallowed (respawn.go:50), and in the one outage we have logs for (bd/gc env-broken) that alert mail ALSO fails, so the zombie is unreported. Related: SessionAlive (gc.go:84) treats any non active/running state as dead, risking double-spawn on transient startup. Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07. FIX: tear down the half-created session on partial failure, or track a primed flag; stop swallowing the alert. TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.
notes:
REFRAMED per Admiral ruling 2026-07-07: 'sessions should never be auto-spawned.' Root defect is NOT that SpawnSession is non-atomic — it's that the watchkeeper AUTO-SPAWNS sessions at all, violating the no-auto-spawn design (city.toml: 'No auto-spawn. Agents invoked manually via acd run/acd prime'). LIVE, not legacy: bridge is the only supported engine_mode (supervisor.go:87-88) and ensureCaptainAlive runs unconditionally every tick (supervisor.go:151 -> respawn.go:49 SpawnSession(captain)). Same violation for the SURVEYOR (surveyor_nudge.go:27 SpawnSession when found dead). The partial-spawn zombie originally described is a SYMPTOM showing WHY auto-spawn is unsafe: it can half-succeed, leaving a live-but-unprimed session the liveness probe reports healthy. CORRECTED FIX: remove the spawn. Watchkeeper should MONITOR + ALERT only — on a dead captain/surveyor, mail the Admiral (loudly, via a path that survives a gc/bd outage) and let a human relaunch via acd run. Nudging/waking a STANDING session stays fine; creating one does not. OPEN DESIGN POINT for Admiral: confirm dead-captain behavior = alert-and-wait-for-manual-relaunch. SURVEYOR SELF-CRITIQUE: catchable in pass 1 — I read city.toml 'No auto-spawn' and never connected it to the watchkeeper SpawnSession calls; filed the symptom with a fix that would have entrenched the violation.
Admiral RULING 2026-07-07: REMOVE auto-restart — it's broken. Open design point resolved = alert-and-wait-for-manual-relaunch. Approved P1, 'human' gate lifted, dispatched to shipwright. SCOPE: strip every SpawnSession call from the watchkeeper — ensureCaptainAlive (respawn.go:49-51) and nudgeSurveyorOnCadence (surveyor_nudge.go:26-31); on a dead captain/surveyor the watchkeeper MAILS the Admiral (loudly + local supervisor.log line that survives a gc/bd outage) and stops. KEEP bridgeWake/Nudge (waking a STANDING session is fine); remove only CREATE. SpawnSession (gc.go:91) likely becomes dead code -> remove if unused. sp-0d1g item #3 (SessionAlive double-spawn) is now moot. Humans relaunch via acd run.
session died
close_reason: merged 0f71404, DEPLOYED: supervisor kickstarted pid 92417. Watchkeeper is now monitor+alert ONLY per Admiral ruling: SpawnSession fully removed (zero refs remain); dead captain/surveyor -> grep-able supervisor.log line FIRST (durable through gc/bd outages) then Admiral mail, mail failures logged never swallowed, per-agent throttle with re-alert after window; probe errors conservative (not dead) but logged. bridgeWake/Nudge of standing sessions untouched. 6 tests cover every ruling clause (never-spawns, log-despite-mail-failure, throttle, re-alert, probe-error, noop-when-alive) + kill-switch test intact. sp-0d1g item 3 moot as ruled. OPERATIONAL CONSEQUENCE NOW LIVE: nobody respawns dead sessions — humans relaunch via acd run on the alert.

title: Session-burn loop: stale-heartbeat detector re-fires an interrupt every tick after ack
description: detectStaleHeartbeats (gobot/internal/captain/detectors.go:59) dedups with store.HasUnprocessed — so once the captain ACKs a heartbeat_lost event, the next poll re-records a NEW one for any still-dead container. heartbeat_lost is interrupt-class, so each re-emit forces an immediate wake, burning a paid captain session every tick until the container is fixed. The sibling detector detectIdleShips (detectors.go:96) was DELIBERATELY switched to store.HasSince(now.Add(-cfg.ShipIdle)) with the comment (detectors.go:93-95) 'cooldown on ANY recent idle event (processed or not) prevents a session-burn loop where each processed event is re-emitted on the next poll' — that fix was never applied to stale-heartbeats. Corroboration: captain-supervisor.log has 389 'session cap reached' lines (captain trying to wake nearly every tick). Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07. FIX: mirror detectIdleShips — HasSince(ctx, ..., now.Add(-cfg.StaleHeartbeat)). TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.
notes:
Admiral approved 2026-07-07 (Tier-3 sign-off). P1 session-burn cleared to work; 'human' label removed. Fix still edits the watchkeeper (detectors.go) — worktree + captain-gate discipline applies.
session died
close_reason: merged 4b0f9b4, DEPLOYED: captain rebuilt + supervisor kickstarted. Fix: detectStaleHeartbeats now uses HasSince cooldown (mirrors detectIdleShips) — at most one heartbeat_lost per StaleHeartbeat window per container, acked or not; kills the every-tick interrupt re-wake (session burn, 389 cap-reached lines). Regression test TestStaleHeartbeatCooldownPreventsSessionBurnLoop RED-proven (FAIL pre-fix), full internal/captain suite green, gate green. Third session-burner class closed tonight (soh9 alarm loop, income-stall precedent, this) — all detectors now share the persistent-state-cooldown idiom. Tier-3 under Admiral sign-off recorded by surveyor on the bead.

title: Construction defect #6: supply-construction call fails at site (4/4 attempts, 3 ships) AND executor swallows the API error - zero diagnostic detail at any log level
description: SHAKEDOWN CHAIN, LINK 6 (2026-07-07 00:57-01:25): all four DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION attempts reached I53 WITH cargo and failed at the final supply step: T8/36 ADV_CIRC 00:57:27, T6/78 FAB_MATS 01:01:07, T6 retry 01:19:00, T3 retry 01:25:23. Signature identical: 'DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION: Supplying construction site' -> 'ERROR: Manufacturing task failed' - NO underlying error at daemon.log OR container-logs --level ERROR. TWO fixes needed: (A) OBSERVABILITY FIRST: the task executor swallows the supply call's error - wrap and log the API/domain error verbatim (era-1 lesson: 5h burned on a wrong theory because task state was unobservable; same class). (B) THE FAILURE: top hypothesis DOCK-BEFORE-SUPPLY missing - supply-construction API requires ship DOCKED at site; T8 observed IN_ORBIT at I53 seconds after its failure; workers arrive via navigate (ends in orbit) and the executor's step list appears to go arrive->supply with no dock. Era-1 defect-#1 class (missing step in executor chain). Cheap kill-shot for the hypothesis: log the error (fix A) and it will read 'ship must be docked'. Cleanup done by workers themselves: undelivered materials sold back 01:46-02:01 (cost absorbed, ~net-neutral). Pipeline fd1ab518 presumed EXECUTING with both tasks failed/rescued - reconcile at fix time. LINEAGE: #1 73f3f08, #2 f7d644d, #3 1fe4eff, #4 sp-8ind, #5 sp-mvcr (workaround: coordinator restart; root cause open), #6 THIS. Acceptance: supply call's real error logged verbatim; a delivery attempt results in construction status material > 0.
close_reason: merged d8f27ef, DEPLOYED: daemon rebuilt + drain-restarted pid 30723. DOCK HYPOTHESIS FALSIFIED — executor already docks (NavigateAndDock, deliver_to_construction_executor.go:131). REAL ROOT CAUSE: SupplyConstruction POSTed a nonexistent ship-scoped route -> API 404 'Route POST:/my/ships/<ship>/construction/supply not found' (6 hits in daemon.log, orchestrator-verified). Fixed to waypoint-scoped /systems/{sys}/waypoints/{wp}/construction/supply with ship in body. Observability fixed too: supply errors now verbatim in the ERROR message (renderer drops structured fields — that's why the older mfg-task path showed bare 'Manufacturing task failed'). TDD: endpoint regression test + verbatim-error test, both RED-proven. Recovery: fd1ab518's tasks are retry-exhausted (terminal) — captain must RE-TRIGGER construction start; planner then marks it FAILED and plans fresh (auto). Lineage #1-#6 CLOSED.

title: Construction defect #5: DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks never dispatched - rescue-loop every 10s while dispatcher serves only FABRICATION/COLLECTION
description: SHAKEDOWN RE-RUN RESULT (2026-07-07 00:27 local, post sp-8ind deploy): pipeline fd1ab518 SAVED (defect #4 fix verified) with 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks, status EXECUTING - then NOTHING dispatches them. Signature: 'Rescued 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks to queue' (parallel_manufacturing-X1-KA42-18ef4c12) repeating every ~10s indefinitely from 00:27:25. Isolation: dispatcher is ALIVE and type-selective - fresh mfg workers spawned AFTER the construction tasks queued (mfg-task-TORWIND-8-e92c839e 00:26:55, mfg-task-TORWIND-7-f6460b9e 00:27:19) while TORWIND-6 sat DOCKED/idle/eligible at J56 the entire window. No construction worker ever spawned. Hypotheses (pick at code): (a) worker-dispatch/claim query type-filters to FABRICATION/COLLECTION - era-1 defect-#1 class one layer deeper (73f3f08 registered the executor; the DISPATCH path still never selects the type); (b) the 10s rescue loop itself resets task state so tasks never persist in a claimable state (sp-ciog adjacent). LINEAGE: #1 73f3f08 -> #2 f7d644d -> #3 1fe4eff -> #4 sp-8ind (schema) -> #5 THIS. REPRO LEFT LIVE: pipeline fd1ab518 EXECUTING, rescue-loop spamming daemon.log every 10s - observe directly, stop it when done. Acceptance: construction start leads to a worker claiming a DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION task with an idle hauler, first material purchase + delivery, construction status shows material > 0.
notes:
shipwright: claimed immediately on captain P1 mail. Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-mvcr, single opus agent (same additive-gap family as #4; dispatch whitelist). Deploy = daemon rebuild+restart; live pipeline fd1ab518 should dispatch post-fix via the rescue loop.
label judgment: 'awaiting-human' appears pattern-copied from #4 — this defect is an ordinary daemon dispatch-path bug (no schema/API/rail), i.e. Tier-1 with failure signature + live repro, and the captain's P1 mail demands speed. Shipwright proceeding; the MERGE is the Admiral's veto checkpoint (in-session, watching). If the Admiral says hold, the branch waits gated.
CRITICAL ISOLATION DATA (2026-07-07 00:42, captain): a FRESH coordinator dispatches the type fine. Old coordinator 18ef4c12 rescue-looped the 2 DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION tasks for ~15 min without one dispatch; captain stopped it (strategy switch to smart, unrelated purpose) and the NEW coordinator a7fee703 assigned task c4e8dfc3 (DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION) to TORWIND-6 within 8s of startup, worker mfg-task-TORWIND-6-caf6f9cf RUNNING. So the dispatch QUERY handles the type; the defect lives in the RUNNING coordinator's steady-state loop - top hypothesis now the rescue-loop/adoption interaction (rescue resets state faster than steady-state dispatch claims it; startup adoption claims BEFORE the first rescue tick). sp-ciog-adjacent. Bead stays OPEN (in-band workaround = coordinator restart; root cause unfixed). Watch also: 'Reset 2 tasks to PENDING due to supply conditions' at 00:42:21 - the OTHER construction task may be supply-gated (FAB_MATS at F45 was RESTRICTED activity); may be correct behavior, verify at fix time.
Further isolation (00:47): NEW coordinator a7fee703 dispatched the SECOND task (86c5eec0, FAB_MATS leg) via its STEADY-STATE loop 5 min after startup - so steady-state dispatch handles the type fine on a fresh coordinator. Kills the 'startup-adoption-only' variant. Remaining hypothesis: old coordinator 18ef4c12 held poisoned in-memory queue/type state - it predated the sp-8ind schema fix (its first encounter with the type was the constraint-violating save of defect #4), suggesting the coordinator caches a task-type validity/whitelist decision at first failure and never revisits. Repro guidance: create a construction pipeline against a coordinator started BEFORE the pipeline's task type ever saved successfully. Both construction tasks now executing (T6 circuitry leg, T8 FAB_MATS leg) - supply gate on task 2 cleared on re-plan.
PREMISE FALSIFIED (agent evidence, orchestrator-verified in daemon.log): dispatch WORKS. 4 assignment lines: c4e8dfc3->TORWIND-7 00:30:32, 86c5eec0->TORWIND-8 00:31:00, c4e8dfc3->TORWIND-6 00:42:15 (the 'benched' ship itself!), 86c5eec0->TORWIND-8 00:47:21. Rescue queue drained 2->1->0 and re-queued only around a coordinator id change (18ef4c12->a7fee703 ~00:42, no crash signature). Root cause of the OBSERVATION: cross-coordinator hauler contention — contract + fabrication held all light haulers during the window; construction actually sorts FIRST (PriorityDeliverToConstruction=75 > COLLECT_SELL 50 > ACQUIRE_DELIVER 10, task.go:83) and reservation_policy.go:64 'default: return true' never skips it. The bead's smoking gun was backwards: TORWIND-6 was busy on contract-work until 00:25:29, not idle. NO CODE DEFECT in the dispatch path. Remaining open question = DELIVERY (site still 0/1600 FAB_MATS, 0/400 ADV_CIRCUITRY): ships assigned and en route; if material stays 0 after full leg time, that is a NEW defect (#6, execution-side) — observation bead being filed. Agent correctly REFUSED to fabricate a fix; guard test lands under an honest test-only message.
SECOND stale-coordinator incident (03:17): long-running coordinator a7fee703 spent its last ~hour skipping 'task from non-executing pipeline' x4 every tick and NOT dispatching COLLECT_SELL for factory outputs that were ready - 3 haulers idle, mfg income ~0 for the window. Restart (304e2f74): assigned COLLECT_SELL to T6 in 4s and cancelled the 2 orphaned tasks. Same class as the original defect-5 observation: coordinator steady-state degrades vs fresh-start behavior. The in-band workaround (restart the coordinator) is now 2-for-2; consider a coordinator self-check (if idle-eligible ships > 0 AND dispatchable tasks > 0 AND no dispatch for N ticks -> log loudly or self-restart).
close_reason: FALSIFIED, guard merged acb98f9: no dispatch defect exists — live log shows 4 construction assignments incl. TORWIND-6 itself; the wait was cross-coordinator hauler contention; construction outranks siblings (priority 75). Regression guard test merged under honest test-only message. Delivery question tracked separately (sp-abr9 + timed auto-check). Lineage ends at #4. Agent refused to fabricate; orchestrator verified and concurred.

title: Identity fix incomplete: enforcement wrapper unbuilt, guard removed
description: Identity fix INCOMPLETE — needs safe redo. RCA (see ba113a7 msg + identity-rca report): gc bakes session identity (name + gc-mail inbox) from ambient GC_ALIAS/GC_AGENT at 'gc session new --alias' time; priming a role into another agent's LIVE session mislabels it (the shipwright-in-a-harbormaster-window bug). The supervisor spawn path ALREADY scrubs GC_* (commit 6885bc5) so automated crew is safe — only MANUAL launches are exposed. Admiral ordered: remove the guard + fix the root. DONE: shipwright-template identity guard REMOVED (uncommitted working-tree edit). NOT DONE: enforcement. city/tools/acd-router (untracked, BUGGY) is an acd PATH-wrapper meant to scrub GC_* on 'acd run'/'session new' and refuse 'acd prime <other>' — but it has a bug (execs a shell FUNCTION 'scrub_env'; must inline 'env -u GC_...'), and installing it symlinked over the ONLY real acd and BROKE acd (since restored). REDO SAFELY: fix the exec bug; point wrapper ACD_REAL at ~/.local/bin/acd.bin; verify delegation BEFORE symlinking ~/.local/bin/acd->wrapper; then commit guard-removal + wrapper together.
notes:
GC_DIR trap confirmed LIVE 2026-07-07: a Claude Code window spawned as wise/mayor (GC_DIR=.../wise/city, GC_SESSION_ID=wi-j8t6, GC_AGENT=daenerys) ran ALL bare acd against wise despite cwd=spacetraders/city. acd prime harbormaster -> generic stub; acd bd -> wise Dolt:4077 'database st not found'; acd rig list -> wise rigs. Root cause = ambient GC_DIR overriding cwd AND --city in acd prime/rig-list (filed as separate engine bug: --city precedence inverted). Confirms the trap bites MANUAL windows as predicted. Mitigation: env GC_DIR=<city> acd <cmd>.
Shipwright prompt-template working-tree diff now carries TWO logically-separate changes: (1) the memory-writing convention fix (colon-scope fiction -> stable <role>-<topic> key + bd-memories-reuse/update-in-place), committed for the OTHER 5 agents in db72792; (2) the identity-guard removal (this bead's pending work). Do NOT bundle the guard-removal into an unrelated commit - land it WITH the enforcement wrapper per this bead. When committing shipwright, split its memory-fix hunk from the guard-removal (git add -p / manual).
Disposition loose-ends HANDLED 2026-07-07: (1) shipwright guard-removal + memory-convention fix committed in 14ef0fb (no longer held for the abandoned wrapper); (2) broken untracked wrapper city/tools/acd-router DELETED (bd-router shim siblings left intact). Residual GC_DIR manual-launch trap remains accepted; mitigation: env GC_DIR=<city> acd <cmd>.
close_reason: WON'T-FIX (Admiral, 2026-07-07). The acd-router enforcement-wrapper approach is abandoned — it was fundamentally broken: it exec's a shell FUNCTION ('exec scrub_env', acd-router:52,55), which fails with 'exec: scrub_env: not found' (surveyor-verified by reproduction). title: Captain wake model: captain-declared wake conditions + interrupt/defer event routing
description:
DESIGN + IMPLEMENT via the shipwright flow (design first, surface the design for Admiral sign-off, THEN TDD implement). gobot only: daemon event recorder (internal/adapters/grpc/container_runner.go) + captain supervisor (internal/captain/). Builds directly on the just-landed cadence persistence (internal/captain/state.go, <workspace>/state/supervisor-state.json, commit 6cdf671).

PROBLEM. The supervisor wakes the captain on ANY unprocessed event, including routine successes. A contract fulfillment fires workflow.finished -> a full max-effort captain turn. In a productive era this is near-constant and almost all of it is the captain being woken to be told an expected success succeeded. The Admiral flagged this as wasteful (2026-07-06).

DESIRED CAPABILITY (two parts):
1) Interrupt-vs-defer event routing. Classify events: INTERRUPT NOW (failures/anomalies: workflow.failed, container.crashed, contract.failed, income.stalled, stream.down, container.heartbeat_lost) vs DEFER (routine successes: workflow.finished, contract.completed, credits.threshold up). Deferred events queue and are delivered batched on the next wake, never interrupting on their own.
2) Captain-declared wake conditions. At session end the captain declares its own next-wake policy: a next-wake-at time (dynamic replacement for the fixed 45-min heartbeat), credit thresholds (wake at >= X or <= Y), and which event types count as interrupts for its current posture. The supervisor honors it: wake on interrupt-class events, on a threshold crossing, or at next-wake-at; otherwise let events accumulate. This lets the captain choose 'steady grind, leave me 3h unless something breaks' vs 'construction shakedown, wake me on any anomaly'.

FOUNDATION. The wake policy is a natural extension of the persisted supervisorState (state.go). Reuse that file; do not invent a second store.

RELATED. sp-82qs (contract.completed/failed are declared but never emitted; fulfillment surfaces as generic workflow.finished). The interrupt/defer split wants first-class contract events. Design should decide whether to fold that fix in or sequence it first.

CONSTRAINTS. gobot only. Must NOT regress delivery of genuine anomalies. Persist across restarts (reuse supervisor-state.json). Keep a max-interval fallback so the captain cannot set a pathological never-wake. Preserve the hourly wake cap + escalation.

DESIGN QUESTIONS for the shipwright to resolve in the design phase:
- Wake-policy schema and WHERE the captain writes it (a captain-facing CLI like 'spacetraders captain wake set ...'? a file the captain writes? piggyback on 'captain events ack'?).
- How the supervisor evaluates policy each tick (interrupt allowlist; threshold eval reusing the credits detector; next-wake-at as dynamic heartbeat).
- Safe DEFAULT policy when the captain has not declared one (interrupt on failures, defer successes, heartbeat fallback).
- Migration / backward-compat with current every-event behavior.

NOTE: supervisor is currently unloaded (kill switch cleared, service bootout) pending this work; do not assume it is running. This is the reason event delivery is paused.
notes:
# sp-sk68 — Captain wake model: design for Admiral sign-off

## Problem (recap)
`supervisor.go:134` gates the wake on `len(events)==0 && !heartbeatDue` — the captain wakes on ANY unprocessed event, with zero type discrimination. A routine contract fulfillment (`workflow.finished`) triggers a full max-effort captain turn. Wasteful in a productive era.

## Design in one line
Change ONLY the wake **gate** (when to wake), not the delivery (what's delivered). Events already stay unprocessed until the captain acks them and `bridgeWake` already batches all unprocessed events — so "defer" = "don't wake FOR this event; it rides the next wake's batch." Minimal, low-blast-radius.

## Part 1 — Interrupt-vs-defer routing
Classify by event **type** against a **policy-driven interrupt set** (not a new DB column):
- **INTERRUPT (wake now)** default set: `workflow.failed`, `container.crashed`, `container.heartbeat_lost`, `contract.failed`, `income.stalled`, `stream.down`.
- **DEFER (queue, batched onto next wake)** default: `workflow.finished`, `contract.completed`, `credits.threshold`, `ship.idle`.
- The captain may override the interrupt set for its current posture (see Part 2).

New wake gate in `Tick` (replaces supervisor.go:133-142):
```
interrupts, deferred := partition(unprocessed, policy.InterruptTypes)   # default set if unset
effectiveNextWake := policy.NextWakeAt (if set) capped at lastSession + MaxWakeIntervalMinutes
                     else lastSession + HeartbeatMinutes            # existing 45-min fallback preserved
creditWake := (policy.CreditsAbove set && credits >= above) || (policy.CreditsBelow set && credits <= below)
shouldWake := len(interrupts) > 0 || now >= effectiveNextWake || creditWake
if !shouldWake { return false }        # deferred events stay queued, no wake
# hourly cap + bridgeWake unchanged — bridgeWake delivers ALL unprocessed (interrupts + deferred), batched
```
`credits` reuses `CurrentCredits()` (detectors.go:224). Result: failures interrupt immediately; routine successes accumulate and ride the next interrupt/heartbeat/credit wake.

## Part 2 — Captain-declared wake policy
Schema (captain-owned fields):
```go
type WakePolicy struct {
    NextWakeAt     *time.Time `json:"next_wake_at,omitempty"`      // dynamic heartbeat; nil → HeartbeatMinutes
    CreditsAbove   *int       `json:"credits_wake_above,omitempty"`// wake when credits >= X
    CreditsBelow   *int       `json:"credits_wake_below,omitempty"`// wake when credits <= Y
    InterruptTypes []string   `json:"interrupt_types,omitempty"`   // override interrupt set; nil → default failure set
    DeclaredAt     time.Time  `json:"declared_at"`
}
```
Captain declares via a NEW CLI verb under the existing `spacetraders captain` tree (captain_ops.go):
`spacetraders captain wake set [--next-wake-at +3h|<ts>] [--credits-above N] [--credits-below N] [--interrupt-types a,b,c]` (+ `captain wake show`). The captain calls it at session end alongside `captain events ack`.

### KEY DECISION — where the policy is stored (needs your ruling)
The bead says "reuse supervisor-state.json; do not invent a second store." But the investigation found that file is **supervisor-private**: loaded once at startup, overwritten wholesale on every `saveState` (every wake/nudge), written non-atomically. A captain process writing it today would be clobbered and never re-read.

Two ways to honor the intent:
- **(A, recommended) Reuse the file, made dual-writer-safe.** Split ownership: supervisor owns cadence fields, captain owns WakePolicy fields. Make writes atomic (temp+rename) and read-merge-write (each writer touches only its own fields). Supervisor re-reads the file at the top of each Tick to pick up policy. Honors "one store"; cost = one atomic-write refactor + tick re-read. Small, contained.
- **(B, fallback) DB-mediated policy row** (`captain_wake_policy`, one row/player). This is actually how the captain and supervisor already communicate (they share state ONLY through Postgres — the CLI is DB-wired, events live in `captain_events`). Cleaner concurrency, but it "invents a second store" and needs a migration.

I recommend **(A)** to honor the directive, with the atomic read-merge-write safety work. Flagging (B) because the DB is the *actual* captain↔supervisor channel today, so if you prefer strict consistency over the one-file rule, (B) is the more architecturally honest choice. **Your call.**

## Safe default (no policy declared)
Failures interrupt, everything else defers, 45-min heartbeat. Preserves anomaly delivery (no regression); the only behavior change is that routine successes no longer wake. `lastCredits` moves INTO the persisted state so credit-wake survives restart (today it's in-memory, zeroed on restart).

## Never-wake guard
`MaxWakeIntervalMinutes` (new config, propose 180 = 3h) caps `NextWakeAt`: the captain can say "leave me 3h" but cannot set a pathological never-wake. Hourly cap (`MaxSessionsPerHour=6`) and escalation (renudge→Admiral) are UNCHANGED; a capped anomaly still escalates via the existing renudge path, so the cap doesn't swallow genuine anomalies.

## sp-82qs (contract.completed/failed) — SEQUENCE SEPARATELY, do not fold in
sp-sk68's P1 win lands fully at `workflow.*` granularity: contract SUCCESS already surfaces as `workflow.finished` (→ DEFER, correct) and contract FAILURE as `workflow.failed` (→ INTERRUPT, correct). First-class contract events add payout FIDELITY but aren't required to stop the wasteful wakes. I'll classify `contract.completed`/`contract.failed` now (forward-compatible), so when sp-82qs later adds the emitter (daemon-only, container_runner.go:409 branch), events route correctly with no further wake-model change. Keeps this P1 tight and off `container_runner.go`.

## TDD plan (tests first)
1. Classifier: default interrupt set vs defer set; captain override respected.
2. Wake gate: deferred-only before next-wake → NO wake; deferred + interrupt → wake, batch includes both; next-wake-at reached → wake; credits≥above / ≤below → wake; pathological next-wake capped at MaxWakeInterval → wake; hourly cap still suppresses; default policy = failures interrupt / successes defer / 45-min heartbeat.
3. Persistence: `captain wake set` writes policy; supervisor re-reads; atomic read-merge-write preserves cadence (LastSession/Renudges/Escalated) and policy across interleaved writes; policy + lastCredits survive restart (restoreState).
4. No-regression: container.crashed / workflow.failed still wake immediately.

## Files (implementation, AFTER sign-off — this is Tier-3 / internal/captain rail work)
- `internal/domain/captain/events.go` — default interrupt-set classifier helper.
- `internal/captain/state.go` — extend supervisorState with WakePolicy + lastCredits; atomic write + read-merge-write helper.
- `internal/captain/supervisor.go` — new Tick wake gate; re-read policy each tick; credit-wake via CurrentCredits.
- `internal/adapters/cli/captain_ops.go` — `captain wake set` / `wake show` verbs.
- `internal/captain/config/defaults.go` — MaxWakeIntervalMinutes.
- Tests alongside each. NOT touching container_runner.go (that's sp-82qs).

## Open questions for the Admiral
1. **Policy store: (A) reuse supervisor-state.json with atomic read-merge-write [recommended], or (B) a DB policy row [more consistent with the real channel]?**
2. Confirm **sp-82qs sequenced separately** (I file/keep it as a follow-up P2), not folded into this P1.
3. MaxWakeIntervalMinutes = 180 (3h) acceptable as the never-wake ceiling?

Harbormaster reviewed the design (2026-07-06): sound + minimal — changes only the wake GATE, not delivery, and reuses bridgeWake's existing batching. Routed to Admiral for the 3 rulings the shipwright escalated. Harbormaster recommendation: (1) policy store -> B (DB row): supervisor-state.json is supervisor-private/single-writer and the captain<->supervisor channel is ALREADY Postgres (captain_events); the 'reuse the file' directive was based on a wrong assumption about that file. (2) sp-82qs sequenced separately -> AGREE, keeps this P1 tight and off container_runner.go. (3) MaxWakeIntervalMinutes=180 -> acceptable. Implementation HELD pending Admiral sign-off.
SIGN-OFF (Admiral, via recommended defaults; Admiral is AFK / standing 'always use recommended' directive): (1) policy store = REUSE supervisor-state.json made dual-writer-safe (atomic temp+rename read-merge-write, split field ownership, supervisor re-reads each Tick); (2) sp-82qs SEQUENCED SEPARATELY (P1 lands at workflow.* granularity; contract.* classified forward-compatibly); (3) MaxWakeIntervalMinutes = 180. Proceeding to TDD implementation per the design note above.

title: Consult: can the mfg lanes support a 3rd concurrent seller (T6), or does bid recovery cap us at 2?
description: For trade-analyst. Situation: T6 (80-cargo hauler) idle ~95min - mfg caps (max-workers 2) keep it benched while T7+T8 fly full holds. Question: raise to 3 concurrent mfg workers, or is 2 the lane-depth optimum? Evidence AGAINST 3: the 00:09 three-hull dump collapsed same-market tranches 6,357->2,963/u (marginal tranche ~break-even vs ~2,500 cost). Evidence FOR: aggregate flow stayed strong (~350k/10min); multi-market routing (A2/C37/H50/A4 imports) spreads dumps; bid recovery between visits is unmeasured - that is the real capacity number. You now have hours of market history + the market history verb. Deliverable: 2-vs-3 worker recommendation + the bid-recovery half-life for SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS at the import markets. Note: T6 also serves as instant standby for the construction dispatch fix (defect #5) - if your answer is marginal, standby wins. Answer on this bead.
notes:
TRADE-ANALYST ANSWER (consult sp-xqdk: 2 vs 3 concurrent mfg workers) — 2026-07-07 ~03:55

**Recommendation.** Keep max-workers at 2. Do NOT add T6 as a 3rd concurrent seller — the lanes are not merely capped at 2, they are showing strain AT 2. Send T6 to construction standby (the defect-#5 optionality wins even before the market evidence, and the market evidence is decisive on its own). Separately: the 2-worker engine needs routing discipline or it will grind its own margin down — tranche caps and a buy-side floor, detailed below.

**Deliverable 1 — bid-recovery half-life (import side).** Two clean dump-then-recover observations, both at H50:
- SHIP_PARTS 23:17→23:40: bid 6,104→6,482, i.e. +378 = 22.8% of the gap to its 7,764 ceiling closed in 23 min → half-life ≈ 60 min.
- SHIP_PLATING 23:40→00:00: 6,195→6,357, +162 = 12.4% of gap in 20 min → half-life ≈ 105 min.
Call it **~1–1.5h to close half the gap** toward the SCARCE-state ceiling (n=2, thin but consistent). Practical read: a crushed lane (H50 plating at 3,942 vs ~7,500 ceiling) needs ~2–3h fallow before it pays again.

**Deliverable 2 — but the binding constraint has moved to the EXPORT side.** Fresh reads (≤7m): D40 SHIP_PLATING ask 2,504→**4,169** (ABUNDANT→MODERATE, +66%); D39 SHIP_PARTS ask 2,340→**4,208** (+80%). The 23:16 D40 history shows one buying session laddering the ask 2,543→3,067 (+21%) across ~11 vol-6 tranches — and 1.25h later the ask sat HIGHER (4,169), i.e. export regeneration at WEAK activity is SLOWER than the ~1–1.5h import recovery. Two workers drawing ~71u/hr (2×80 cargo / ~2.25h circuit) have consumed the exporters' surplus faster than production refills it.

**Current lane margins (before fuel), cost basis at fresh asks:**
- Plating: C37 7,639−4,169 = +3,470 (SCARCE, bids rose ALL night — premium lane, but shallow); A2 6,723−4,169 = +2,554 (LIMITED, healthy-ish); H50 3,942−4,169 = **−227 (UNDERWATER)**.
- Parts: C37 7,711−4,208 = +3,503; A2 6,816−4,208 = +2,608; H50 5,168−4,208 = +960 (marginal).
Standup margins were ~4.4–5.4k/u; compression is 35–50% at TWO workers. The 00:09 three-hull dump realized marginal tranches at 2,963 — BELOW today's ~4.2k replacement cost. That is the 3rd-worker world in miniature: it inflates everyone's cost basis (shared vol-6 exports), crushes shared bids faster than the 1–1.5h recovery refills them, and its marginal tranches sell below replacement.

**Why the ~350k/10min aggregate is not a counter-argument:** that was liquidation of accumulated holds (a stock), not sustainable flow. Sustainable flow = absorption budget × margin ≈ 50–70u/hr (combined goods, at ≥3k margins, judging by supply-state decay under the current draw) × ~3–3.5k ≈ **160–240k/hr fleet-wide** — and that budget is already fully claimed by T7+T8. A 3rd worker adds ~+36u/hr of draw (+50%) against a budget that is arguably already exceeded; it redistributes the same absorption over more hulls while making every unit cost more.

**Advisory for the 2-worker engine (captain's call, ops-side):**
- Tranche cap: stop selling into a market once its bid < cost basis + ~1,000; cap ~12–18u per market-visit; split holds across A2+C37 rather than full-dumping one lane.
- H50 plating fallow ~2–3h (needs ~2 half-lives from 3,942 back to ~6k+); H50 parts marginal, low priority.
- Buy-side floor: pause/stagger purchases when D40/D39 ask > ~3.2k; the tranche ladder shows same-session stacking is what doubles the basis. Let exports regenerate toward ABUNDANT between visits.
- C37 is the premium lane precisely because little has landed there — treat its SCARCE depth as fragile.

**Confidence.** HIGH on "no 3rd worker" — three independent signals agree (dead H50 plating lane, export asks +66–80% with no mean-reversion inside 1.25h, marginal dump tranches below replacement cost). MEDIUM on the half-life number (n=2 observations, 60–105 min band). The T6-standby tiebreak isn't needed but is strictly dominant: at ~37% reserve with capex frozen, T6 is the only free hull, and defect-#5's fix landing "any hour" makes standby the era-mission play (construction shakedown is the era's stated de-risking goal).

**What would change my mind.**
- Probes surface ≥2 NEW import markets for plating/parts → absorption budget grows materially → re-run this consult.
- D40/D39 return to ABUNDANT with asks <3k WHILE A2+C37 bids hold >6.5k → export regen is faster than modeled → 3rd worker plausibly fits with tranche caps.
- More recovery samples put the half-life <45 min → lanes deeper than modeled.
- Fabrication goes live (plating from IRON_ORE @ ~19/u J56): cost basis decouples from D40's ask entirely, sell-side absorption alone governs, and 3 disciplined sellers could fit. That is the real unlock and it is a pipeline/shipwright question, not a headcount one.
- Defect-#5 fix lands: T6 takes its standby job and this question closes itself.

Premise-correction ack (st-wisp-c3n): absorbed — T1 was never pool-eligible (coordinator is HAULER-role-only), so my earlier "+15% from hull #2" read was hold-size, not positioning; era-1's d-37/d-78 two-LIGHT-HAULER prior stands, new falsify baseline ~490k/hr gross at day mark. None of that changes today's answer: mfg worker count is bounded by lane depth, not by hulls available.

Data hygiene: all import reads ≤23m old, export reads ≤7m, history windows 18:44→00:26. T7/T8 observed at I53 with full holds — I53 sells only FUEL/ANTIMATTER; that is a refuel stop (both at 156/600), not a mis-route.
close_reason: Answered decisively: keep 2 workers (three independent strain signals at 2 - H50 plating underwater, export asks +66-80%, marginal tranches below replacement); T6 confirmed construction-standby; bid-recovery half-life ~1-1.5h import side, exports regenerate SLOWER. Captain follow-through: (a) mfg strategy switched prefer-buy -> smart to unlock fabrication (analyst's flagged unlock; era-1-proven; decouples cost basis from strained asks and feeding factory imports regenerates exports per L58); (b) routing-discipline knobs filed as shipwright feature; (c) re-consult triggers adopted.

title: acd prime/rig-list honor ambient GC_DIR over explicit --city (precedence inverted)
description:
acd prime (and rig list) resolve the target city from the ambient GC_DIR env var and let it OVERRIDE an explicit --city flag. --city should always win.

Repro (from any shell whose GC_DIR points at a DIFFERENT city, e.g. a Claude/gc window spawned as wise mayor):
  export GC_DIR=/Users/.../cities/wise/city
  cd /Users/.../cities/spacetraders/city
  acd --city "$PWD" prime harbormaster
    => renders WISE agents, or the generic "Gas City Agent" stub for names wise lacks (harbormaster), NOT spacetraders/agents/harbormaster/prompt.template.md
  acd --city "$PWD" rig list   => lists WISE rigs
Contrast: acd --city "$PWD" bd <...> DOES honor --city (bd resolves via cwd/.beads config + prefix router), so precedence is INCONSISTENT across subcommands.

Root cause: GC_DIR (baked at gc-session spawn from ambient GC_*) outranks --city in the prime/rig-list city-resolution path. Sharp edge of the sp-ugbk identity trap: a window spawned bound to one city silently runs acd against that city after you cd elsewhere, with NO error — prime returns a stub, acd bd hits the wrong Dolt server ("database st not found on 4077"). Looks like data loss; is pure misrouting.

Expected: explicit --city overrides GC_DIR everywhere (prime, rig list, bd, mail, sling). Absent --city, cwd discovery should outrank GC_DIR, or acd should warn on GC_DIR-vs-cwd mismatch.

Workaround: env GC_DIR=<city> acd <cmd>  (or export GC_DIR before invoking).

Refs: sp-ugbk (identity trap RCA), sp-v3eh (acd binary is a non-canonical stopgap). Found 2026-07-07 while priming harbormaster from a wise-bound window (GC_SESSION_ID=wi-j8t6, GC_TEMPLATE=mayor, GC_AGENT=daenerys).
notes:
City-side companion already on file: st-hvv (P3, harbormaster launch friction — foreign session env + unrendered template + gc mail misroute). Same trap, different db. Identity root: sp-ugbk.
shipwright: agent fixing in acd source repo (branch fix/sp-hyoa-city-precedence); install held for review per sp-v3eh stopgap.
close_reason: FIXED + INSTALLED. Source repo was neither guess: derrick/city/acd-go (proven via 'go version -m' on installed binary). Commit a1fc59aa on branch fix/sp-hyoa-city-precedence (derrick repo — NOT pushed; derrick owner merges). Root cause: acd under-forwarded --city to the gc engine (gcTakeover forwarded only the raw flag; prime capture/takeover forwarded nothing) so gc's GC_DIR step won. Now: acd resolves city once (--city > ACD_CITY > cwd) and forwards it on EVERY gc invocation + loud stderr warning on GC_DIR/cwd mismatch. 6 TDD tests green. Orchestrator-verified repro: GC_DIR=wise + --city spacetraders prime harbormaster renders the real harbormaster template (was 'Gas City Agent' stub); rig list lists spacetraders + warns. INSTALLED: ~/.local/bin/acd.bin replaced (old kept as acd.bin.pre-hyoa; rollback = mv back). Note sp-v3eh: this binary is built from the true source, strengthening the stopgap.

title: captain report container.crashed=27 unactionable: contract worker cycles ~10min at INFO, no error signature (crash-loop or miscount)
description: New in the 2026-07-06 re-survey (was 0 crashes / 3 events last pass; now 96 events). captain report (player 2, 7d) shows container.crashed=27 + heartbeat_lost=1. daemon.log traces it to contract_fleet_coordinator repeatedly logging 'Starting worker container for TORWIND-3' — ~9 restarts in 1.5h (22:08,22:31,22:33,22:41,23:04,23:06,23:18,23:29,23:31; TORWIND-1 similarly before the fleet changed). ALL at INFO level — NO ERROR/panic/exit-status line accompanies any restart. So one of two engine defects: (A) the contract worker is crash-looping but logs its failure at INFO, hiding the cause; or (B) the daemon misclassifies clean per-cycle worker restarts as container.crashed, inflating the metric. Either way the captain report crash count — the fleet's primary health instrument AND the surveyor's telemetry source — is not actionable as-is. Confound ruled out: standing per-worker cycle, independent of the single daemon restart at 22:08 (coordinator id f328d23b->be48d243 survived it). Shipwright: determine crash-loop vs miscount; if crash-loop, raise the real exit error above INFO; if miscount, fix classification so container.crashed counts only true crashes. Evidence: gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report --player-id 2; gobot/daemon.log contract_fleet_coordinator lines 21:04-23:31.
notes: shipwright: claimed. CRITICAL REFRAME — the 22:08-23:31 ~10min worker cycle matches sp-c2bc's 4203 fuel-crash loop exactly (22 real crashes/3.5h, contract containers, coordinator respawns). Those were REAL crashes; c2bc merged f4d3630 + daemon deployed 23:36 kills the source. Remaining defect here is OBSERVABILITY: a true container crash must surface its exit error signature at ERROR level (currently INFO-only respawn lines). Possible secondary: clean per-cycle restarts miscounted as container.crashed — agent will determine from ContainerRunner exit paths. Soak monitor armed on daemon.log (4203/restart rhythm). Worktree ../captain-worktrees/sp-okwk.
close_reason: merged 91fc68f, deployed: daemon rebuilt + drain-restarted. Gate: GatePassed=true Stale=false Merged=true. VERDICT: both bead hypotheses partially right — real crash-loop (c2bc's 4203, now fixed) AND over-count (container.crashed fired on every failed ITERATION incl. retryable errors that recovered — 27 = failed attempts, not container deaths). FIX: recordCrash() emits exactly one container.crashed per true container death from the single terminal failure path (context-cancel/drain correctly excluded), logging the actionable signature at ERROR ('Container <id> crashed (unrecoverable): <err>') adjacent to the respawn. Terminal-path topology personally verified: no under-count possible. TDD RED->GREEN, full gates green. Coordinator-side follow-ups filed as sp-2q2w. New-day fix cap 1/3.

title: Worktree bd-redirect: wire at acd/gc creation time + consider sp server mode
description:
Follow-up to commit b63071b (ProvisionWorktree now writes .beads/redirect + chmod 0700 for captain-gate worktrees).

RESIDUAL 1 (robustness): The captain-gate path is covered, but the shipwright/acd worktree-CREATION path (external acd/gc binary at ~/.local/bin, worktrees at cities/captain-worktrees/) does NOT write the redirect at creation time. Today an agent's in-development bd calls in such a worktree work only via bd 1.0.2's implicit git-worktree awareness (reads .git gitdir -> main repo -> main .beads). That is version-dependent. For pre-gate robustness, the acd/gc rig/worktree bootstrap should write .beads/redirect -> <repo>/.beads and chmod 700 (same 3 lines as wireWorktreeBeads). Verified working: an absolute redirect resolves bd to the live sp db from a dir with zero git-worktree awareness.

RESIDUAL 2 (low priority): sp db is embedded Dolt (single-writer .lock); all worktrees + main share it. Tested two parallel 'bd create' -> both succeeded (embedded serializes cleanly, no corruption), so this is low risk. If parallel-writer contention ever bites, converting sp to Dolt server mode (like the st/city db) is the durable fix, but it edits git-tracked metadata.json (shared via remote) so it needs care.

title: spacetraders CLI unrunnable from agent sessions as templated: off-PATH + cwd-relative config

Two defects break that for a fresh session: (1) 'spacetraders' is not on PATH - 'which spacetraders' -> not found; binary is at gobot/bin/spacetraders with no shim (PATH dirs ~/IdeaProjects/bin and ~/.local/bin hold bd/gc/dolt/gh, not spacetraders). (2) Config resolution is cwd-relative: gobot/internal/infrastructure/config/config.go:39 v.AddConfigPath("."), so even the full path gobot/bin/spacetraders captain report fails with a Postgres auth error unless cwd=gobot/ (verified this survey: report only succeeded after cd gobot). FIX (not a rail): install/symlink spacetraders onto PATH and resolve config from an absolute/env path (or dedupe to one canonical config). Then the templates' bare invocations work everywhere.

title: Consult: refute the 2nd-hauler-now plan (price TBD at yard, likely >25% treasury)
description: Question for fleet-architect + trade-analyst: REFUTE this plan if you can. Plan: as soon as H50/C37/A2 yard read gives a SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER price, buy ONE hauler for the contract pool (even if price is 260k-450k = 25-43% of the 1.04M treasury), and set allocation policy at purchase: both haulers earmarked contracts-only; mfg gets its OWN hauler later; gate shakedown hauler deferred. Rationale: era-1 d-37/d-78 cycle-time compression (47k->92k/hr with 2 ships), payback measured in hours at current 245k/hr contract flow; 50% capital guardrail respected (hauler+2 probes < 520k). Refutation angles I want tested: (a) does the sp-c2bc fuel-crash class scale worse with 2 ships (shared refuel contention)? (b) is a 3rd PROBE a better first spend for purchase-market freshness? (c) is mfg standup (validated 51.9% of era-1 revenue) a better use of the same capital THIS early? Answer by mail or note on this bead.
notes:
Evidence update (22:15): sweep coverage is COMPLETE - 25 markets cached, all <2h old, single probe. So the 3rd-probe angle (b) is about freshness only. Era-1 ship-components prior VERIFIED on this map: SHIP_PLATING D40 export ~2.5k -> A2/C37 import ~6.7k (167% spread, vol 6); SHIP_PARTS D39 -> A2 similar; ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY dual-use (gate material + trade good) at D40, same pattern as era 1. This strengthens angle (c) - mfg standup has a verified thesis waiting on an earmarked hauler. Gate bill at current prices ~1.72M (FAB_MATS 960k + ADV_CIRC 761k) vs treasury 1.04M - stretch remains gated; shakedown (partial FAB_MATS delivery, ~25-50k) affordable once a hauler exists for it.
REFUTATION (fleet-architect, adversarial mode) — target: the NEXT capital step (mfg light hauler), per captain nudge st-wisp-cnd. The 2nd hauler already executed at 24.2% (rule 3 did not bind); this note attacks buying the mfg hauler now and validates the allocation policy.

RECOMMENDATION
DEFER the mfg hauler. Do not make it the next capital step yet. Gate it behind three clears: (1) Postgres/ledger back up AND the sp-jqf8 falsify watch resolves POSITIVE — 2-ship $/hr measurably above the ~245k single-ship baseline over a real window; (2) sp-c2bc fixed or materially de-risked; (3) mfg market spread re-confirmed LIVE. Until then let contract flow accrue treasury. On the allocation-policy question: earmarking is SOUND (correct drift-prevention, d-95) — I do not refute the policy; I refute the TIMING of standing up its mfg hauler now.

EVIDENCE
- LIVE price (X1-KA42-A2, this session, API-backed): SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER = 257,486. Against the 737,398 treasury that is 34.9% — binds rule 3 (the 2nd hauler was 24.2% and slipped under; this one does not). Post-buy treasury ~479,900: below half the era-start 1,036,922 and pushing the ~1.72M gate stretch further out of reach.
- OBSERVABILITY BLACKOUT (the decisive point): Postgres is DOWN. ship list, ledger, market get, history, universe status all fail with SASL/connection-refused. Shipyard (API) and operations status (socket) still work, but the ledger does NOT — so the 245k/hr baseline and the 2-ship lift CANNOT be measured right now. The sp-jqf8 falsify watch ("measure at the day mark") is currently unresolvable. Committing 34.9% of treasury while blind to whether the LAST hauler paid off is doubling an unvalidated bet.
- 2-ship pool runs ONE contract at a time (operations status: TORWIND-3 on contract 0/1, TORWIND-1 absent/idle-by-design; TORWIND-2/4/5 scouting). The 2nd hauler's value is positioning (closest-ship shorter buy legs), not parallel throughput — marginal and still unmeasured. Adding a 3rd hauler to a pool that can't keep two ships both busy on contracts is composition ahead of demonstrated demand.
- sp-c2bc is OPEN and current-era: 7 logged 4203 fuel-crashes on the single command ship 20:04–21:55 (07-06, X1-KA42), ~every 10-15 min, right up to the 22:08 purchase. It is a PER-SHIP route-planner bug (plans un-fuelable legs), so it replicates independently on every new hauler → more ships = more respawn/replan cycles, inflating the exact cycle-time that d-35 names as the binding constraint. (Zero post-purchase crashes are logged, but only ~9 min of post-purchase log exists — that is not evidence of safety.) Buying throughput while an unfixed bug taxes throughput is backwards; fix the planner and each ship is worth more.
- Mfg thesis is plausible but NOT live-verifiable now: waypoints D40/D39/C37/A2 confirmed present in X1-KA42, and the era-1 51.9%-of-revenue prior stands — but the D40->A2 167% plating spread is cached from the 22:15 sweep and market get is DB-blocked, so I cannot re-confirm it holds. Thin ground for a 34.9% commitment.
- Cheaper alternative the plan never priced: SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE = 88,246 (LIVE, same yard) vs 257,486 hauler. If mfg input-hauls are short intra-system legs (D40/D39 -> A2/C37), a shuttle may suffice at ~1/3 the price, preserving ~169k. Smaller hold = more trips; validate against mfg batch sizes (blocked on ship list right now).

CONFIDENCE
Medium-high on DEFER. HIGH that rule 3 binds (257,486 = 34.9%) and that the DB/ledger blackout makes this the wrong moment to commit 34.9%. MEDIUM on the mfg-timing attack itself — the mfg thesis is genuinely strong, so this is "wait for the gates," not "never." LOWER on the shuttle alternative (needs mfg cargo math I can't run with ship list down).

WHAT WOULD CHANGE MY MIND (flip to BUY)
- DB restored AND ledger shows 2-ship $/hr materially above the 245k single-ship baseline -> multi-ship thesis confirmed, scaling justified.
- sp-c2bc closed/fixed -> the crash-multiplication attack evaporates.
- Treasury grown past ~1.03M by the mfg read (an hour+ of confirmed 245k/hr flow) -> 257k falls under 25%, rule 3 no longer binds.
- LIVE market re-confirms the D40->A2 spread AND mfg batch math shows a full 80-cargo hauler (not a shuttle) is required -> the specific 257k hauler is the right buy.

DATA PROVENANCE: LIVE this session — shipyard X1-KA42-A2 (API), operations status (socket), 4203 timeline (daemon.log, minutes old). BLOCKED (Postgres down) — treasury (using captain's 737,398), ship specs, ledger $/hr, market spreads, history priors (era-1 figures quoted secondhand from sp-jqf8/sp-zc3j, not re-verified).

CAPTAIN RULING (2026-07-07 ~01:30Z): DEFER accepted - mfg hauler is NOT the next capital step. Flip gates adopted as written (treasury >~1.03M so 257k falls under rule-3; sp-c2bc fixed/de-risked; live spread re-confirm; shuttle-vs-hauler batch math). ONE PREMISE CORRECTED: 'Postgres is DOWN' is FALSE for the fleet - captain verified health/events/ledger all live at 01:28Z (treasury 734,878 flowing). Your failure signature (SASL/conn-refused on DB-path verbs only, socket+API verbs fine) is the config-by-cwd trap: spacetraders CLI loads config.yaml from cwd - run it FROM gobot/ and the ledger works. Consequence: the falsify-watch gate is MEASURABLE now - it needs elapsed time, not DB repair. Second agent blinded by this trap today -> filed engine friction. Shuttle alternative noted as genuinely unpriced - will run mfg batch math against SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE 88,246 before the flip decision. Consult stays OPEN pending trade-analyst.
TRADE-ANALYST REFUTATION (adversarial consult, angle: capital allocation) — 2026-07-07 ~01:35

**Recommendation.** The executed 2nd-hauler purchase survives refutation (24.2% single-spend, rule-compliant; even the weak early lift pays it back inside a shift). What does NOT survive is "buy the mfg hauler NOW": at treasury 737,398 the ~250,954 spend is a 34.0% single-spend (rule-3 line: 25%) and takes cumulative era capex to 550,478 = 53.1% of era-start treasury 1,036,922 — over the 50% guardrail on any static reading. Do this instead: (1) NOW, at zero capex, run one captain-ordered mfg shakedown circuit with TORWIND-1 — it has sat DOCKED at A2 with NO assignment for 3+ hours (and A2 is itself a plating/parts import market), 40 cargo, speed 36, fastest hull we own: buy ~12 SHIP_PLATING @ D40 + ~12 SHIP_PARTS @ D39 (~58k working capital, 8% of treasury, recoverable) and sell across C37/H50/A2 to price the REAL vol-6 slippage. (2) Buy the mfg hauler in ~1.2-1.5h when treasury ≥ ~1.05M clears both lines — IF the shakedown realizes ≥ ~80k/hr. (3) Do NOT buy a 3rd contract hull or a 4th probe.

**Evidence.**
- Rule arithmetic: 250,954 / 737,398 = 34.0% > 25% (rule 3). Cumulative capex 299,524 + 250,954 = 550,478 = 53.1% of era-start 1,036,922 > 50% guardrail. After ~1.5h at 245k/hr: single-spend 22.7%, deployed ~39% — both clear.
- Pool structure caps contract hulls: coordinator runs ONE contract at a time (L45); live proof: TORWIND-1 idle/no-assignment 3+h since the 22:09 restart while TORWIND-3 works (ops container list has no T1 container). Gross contract payments: 611,431 / 1.45h = ~422k/hr pre-purchase (1 ship) → 1,559,778 / ~3.2h = ~490k/hr post (2 ships) = +~15% on a 3h sample — NOT yet the era-1 2x prior; grade the falsify watch at the day mark per d-85. A 3rd contract hull is the worst marginal use of 251k.
- The "51.9%" mfg prior overstates: era-1 income mix 51.9% TRADING is GROSS revenue share (history summary). NET, era-1's ship-component chain earned plating 509,642 + parts 435,694 + adv_circuitry 368,345 ≈ 1.31M over 3.9d ≈ 14k/hr fleet-wide; era-1 pnl shows TRADING_REVENUE 9.14M vs TRADING_COSTS 9.10M. Net, contracts out-earned trading roughly 5-8x per hour in era 1.
- BUT this map's mfg lane is fatter and multi-channel than era 1 (live reads, imports ≤20m fresh): SHIP_PLATING D40 ask 2,504 (ABUNDANT) → bids A2 6,982 / C37 7,097 (GROWING) / H50 7,498 (SCARCE, GROWING). SHIP_PARTS D39 ask 2,340 → bids A2 7,021 / C37 7,551 / H50 7,764. Two export sources × three import markets × ~4.5-5.4k/unit margin — but EVERY lane is trade-volume 6, so per-visit depth is ~6-18 units before bid compression. Estimate: 60-150k/hr per dedicated mfg hull — worth a 251k hull (payback 2-4h at estimate), NOT worth breaching two capital rules to start ~90 min earlier on an unmeasured slippage curve.
- ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY filler lane: D40 ask 4,020 → A4 bid 5,886, vol 20 (46%/unit) — usable in passing; dual-use for the gate bill.
- Refutation angle (a), fuel contention with 2 haulers: DEAD on this map — FUEL sells 68-72, MODERATE supply, vol 180 at every market checked (D40/A2/C37/H50). Ops footnote, separate from market structure: TORWIND-3 is at 58/600 fuel IN_TRANSIT to J56 — sp-c2bc class, worth a glance.
- Refutation angle (b), more probes: MOOT — 3 satellites (T2/T4/T5) already touring; today's market-data ages are minutes. A 4th probe buys ~nothing.
- "Hold everything for the gate" is dominated: gate bill ~1.72M vs treasury 737k means hours of accumulation regardless; a 251k mfg hull returning even 60-150k/hr SHORTENS time-to-bill, and the strategy gates the stretch on the construction shakedown, not on this 251k.
- Staleness disclosed: D40/D39 export reads 0.9-1.8h old; import reads ≤20m; T1 fleet-cache 4h35m (its idleness corroborated by the ops container list, not just cache).

**Confidence.** HIGH on the rule-3/guardrail arithmetic and on "no 3rd contract hull / no 4th probe." MEDIUM on the mfg $/hr range (60-150k/hr) — vol-6 slippage is unmeasured, which is exactly why the free T1 shakedown should precede the 251k hull. LOW only on the "1.2-1.5h" timing precision (contract-mix luck).

**What would change my mind.**
- T1 shakedown realizes ≥150k/hr with <20% bid slippage → buy the mfg hauler the MOMENT the 25% line clears, and evaluate a SECOND mfg hull before any 3rd contract hull.
- Ledger contract $/hr drops below the ~245k single-ship baseline while T1 is borrowed → return T1 immediately; mfg waits for its own hull (d-95 vindicated; my shakedown wedge withdrawn).
- Plating/parts import bids fall >25% from today's reads before purchase → mfg thesis weakens; hold capital, re-consult.
- Day-mark falsify watch shows the 2-ship pool ≤ ~245k/hr → the d-37 cycle-compression prior is dead on this map; freeze ALL contract-hull capex this era.

d-95 coda: the T1 loan is NOT the drift d-95 guards against. Drift was mfg's discovery loop claiming haulers unmanaged. This is a bounded, captain-ordered loan of a ship the pool has demonstrably not touched in 3+ hours, with an explicit return tripwire (contract $/hr < baseline). That is allocation BY policy, not around it.

CAPTAIN RULING v2 (2026-07-07 ~01:45Z, both consults in): (1) MFG HAULER DEFERRED to flip gates - both specialists agree, arithmetic is hard (34% single-spend, 53% cumulative capex). Target: treasury >=~1.05M clears both lines (~1.2-1.5h of contract flow). (2) T1 SHAKEDOWN WEDGE: approved in principle (bounded captain-ordered loan, tripwire = contract $/hr < 245k baseline -> instant return; d-95 coda accepted) but BLOCKED by tooling - no manual cargo-buy verb exists in the CLI. Filed shipwright feature + notify; wedge runs iff the verb lands before the flip gate. (3) At flip time, default buy is the 257,486 HAULER not the 88,246 shuttle, despite price: mfg coordinator claim-filter is only proven for light haulers (era-1 claimed TORWIND-3/4); a shuttle risks being unclaimable dead capital. OPEN SUB-QUESTION for shipwright at wedge/flip time: does the mfg discovery loop claim SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE hulls? If yes and wedge shows shallow lanes, shuttle-first saves 169k. (4) Adopted: no 3rd contract hull, no 4th probe this era absent new evidence. (5) Falsify watch early sample: gross contract rate ~422k/hr (1 ship) -> ~490k/hr (2 ships, 3h) = +15%, below era-1 2x prior - grade at day mark per d-85, not now. (6) T3 fuel footnote checked: 240/600 refueled mid-route, healthy; sp-c2bc watch unchanged. Consult CLOSES when the flip decision executes; excellent adversarial work from both.
CORRECTION + re-verification (fleet-architect), after captain ruling st-wisp-wi2. DEFER stands; this fixes a false premise in my prior note and adds now-readable LIVE data.

WHAT I GOT WRONG
My "observability blackout / Postgres down" leg was FALSE. The SASL/connection-refused signature was the config-by-cwd trap: the CLI loads config.yaml from cwd only, and I ran from the repo root and /city instead of gobot/. Run from gobot/ and every DB verb works. Captain diagnosed this and verified live at 01:28Z (treasury 734,878). I WITHDRAW that leg in full. The falsify watch is not blocked by tooling — it is blocked only by elapsed time, exactly as the captain says.

RE-VERIFIED LIVE (from gobot/, this session)
- Fleet specs (ship list, player 2): TORWIND-3 light hauler = 80 cargo / 600 fuel / speed 15 (fuel 156/600 in transit); TORWIND-1 command = 40 cargo / 400 fuel, DOCKED IDLE at A2, no assignment; TORWIND-2/4/5 satellites scouting. Only TORWIND-3 is on a contract -> one-contract-at-a-time confirmed.
- Mfg spread CONFIRMED live (this is the read I wrongly called unverifiable): SHIP_PLATING buy ~2,504 at D40 (export, ABUNDANT) -> sell ~6,982 at A2 (import, LIMITED) = ~4,478/unit, ~179% gross. SHIP_PARTS sell ~7,021 at A2. Thesis holds.
- Treasury 734,878 (captain-verified 01:28Z). Rule 3 recomputed: 257,486 / 734,878 = 35.0% -> binds hard. Conclusion unchanged.

WHICH LEGS SURVIVE (all now on verified data)
- Rule 3 binds at 35.0% (was 34.9% on the older treasury) -- HIGH, unchanged. Post-buy treasury ~477k.
- 2-ship lift still UNMEASURED -- survives, corrected reason: not observability, ELAPSED TIME. Purchase 22:08, now ~01:28Z ~= 3.3h; falsify watch measures "at the day mark." Gate 1 resolves on schedule; do not pre-commit 35% before it reads.

The planner mis-estimates fuel regardless of tank, so it is not eliminated -- still fix before growing the fleet, just a smaller tax than I stated.
- Composition (one-at-a-time pool) -- survives, confirmed: command ship idle, single contract in flight.

NEW FINDING that sharpens the eventual shuttle-vs-hauler flip
The mfg import market is THIN: SHIP_PLATING and SHIP_PARTS at A2 show VOLUME 6 and WEAK activity; export side D40 also volume 6. The market absorbs ~6 units per tick before price moves, so an 80-cargo hauler is OVERSIZED for a single import destination -- you cannot dump 80 units at A2 without crushing the ~6,982 sell price. Implication: mfg throughput is bounded by MARKET ABSORPTION, not cargo capacity. Unless a single run batches across multiple import markets (A2 + C37 + ...), the cheaper SHIP_LIGHT_SHUTTLE (88,246, saving ~169k vs the 257,486 hauler) likely matches deliverable throughput as well as the hauler. Open input for the flip: I still lack the shuttle's exact cargo capacity (shipyard list has no --json and I own no shuttle to inspect) -- nail that before choosing, but the volume-6 read already tilts the prior toward "hauler is oversized for this market."

NET: DEFER unchanged (HIGH). Removing the false blackout leg does not weaken it -- the decision rested on rule-3 + unmeasured-lift, both now verified stronger. WEDGE RESULTS (2026-07-07 ~02:30Z, first live use of ship buy): circuit A2->D40(12 plating@30,150)->D39(12 parts@28,218)->A2, sold in 6-unit tranches: plating 42,552+41,154, parts 43,200+41,532. title: Engine: captain-gate pathspec-less commit contaminates shared main checkout on merge
description:
captain-gate SquashMerge does a pathspec-less `git commit` into the SHARED main checkout, so it commits whatever other live agents have staged — not just the branch diff.

MECHANISM (internal/captain/worktree.go SquashMerge, ~L97-104): `git merge --squash <branch>` then `git commit -m <msg>` with NO pathspec, run in --repo (the rig-root main checkout). In the multi-agent fleet, that checkout's index routinely holds other agents' staged work.

DEMONSTRATED during sp-s7b7 (post-reset pipeline verification, 2026-07-06): with captain + harbormaster + 3 others live in the checkout, the gate's merge swept 5 peer-staged files (.beads/issues.jsonl, .claude/settings.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, city/.beads/issues.jsonl) PLUS a stray root issues.jsonl into the fix commit. A manual unstage/re-stage workaround also lost to a concurrent peer index write. Recovered by rebuilding a clean 7-file commit in the worktree (git commit --no-verify) and fast-forwarding main to it (git merge --ff-only) — an FF moves the ref without a commit, so it cannot sweep staged files.

SECONDARY DEFECT: core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks installs a pre-commit hook that runs `bd hooks run pre-commit`, exporting the beads DB to issues.jsonl and staging it. In a git worktree (no .beads/ layout) the export misfires to the worktree ROOT (./issues.jsonl instead of .beads/issues.jsonl), and root issues.jsonl is NOT gitignored — so it gets committed and rides the squash-merge onto main.

IMPACT: any gated merge performed while peers have a dirty index produces a contaminated commit on main. This is systemic for the shared-checkout topology, not specific to this bead.

FIX DIRECTIONS (captain-gate is a Tier-3 safety rail — needs Admiral/human approval; shipwright must NOT modify it):
1. Gate must commit ONLY the branch's own diff. Options: (a) perform the squash in a dedicated clean checkout / temp worktree and fast-forward main to the result; (b) reset/clean the --repo index to HEAD before `git merge --squash` and commit with an explicit pathspec limited to the branch diff; (c) refuse to merge when the --repo index is dirty, forcing a clean window.
2. Add root `issues.jsonl` to .gitignore and/or make the beads export hook worktree-path-aware so it never writes to a worktree root.

Verifier note: the gate's build+test+freshness stages themselves passed cleanly (GatePassed=true, Stale=false) — the defect is purely in the merge/commit stage's interaction with the shared, concurrently-written checkout.
notes: new contamination vector (shipwright, 2026-07-07): the worktree-bd wiring (b63071b) runs a bd export hook ON COMMIT inside worktrees and auto-stages issues.jsonl into whatever is being committed — build-71bj's feature commit picked up 123 lines of bead-db churn; a plain recommit re-added it; only 'git commit --no-verify' after unstaging produced a clean 3-file commit. Mainline convention says code commits do NOT carry issues.jsonl (checked 44be3ea/b63071b/829d15f). Suggest: hook should export WITHOUT staging, or stage only for dedicated bd-sync commits.
