title: Fleet strategy: era torwind-2026-07-05
description:
# Fleet strategy: era torwind-2026-07-05

*Harbormaster draft for Admiral blessing (runbook Phase 7). Standing strategy for the era-2 captain — you read this at prime in a FRESH universe. You know the tooling; you know NOTHING about this map. Everything map-specific below is a PRIOR to verify, not a fact. Server reset 2026-07-05; NEXT reset 2026-07-12T13:00Z, so this era is AT MOST ~6 days and likely less depending on when you register. Weekly cadence is the new normal and it bounds every mission decision. On registration, name the era `torwind-<universe-resetDate>` (this universe: `torwind-2026-07-05`).*

## Mission posture — the Admiral's headline decision

Era 1 earned an A and missed its mission an F: the jump gate finished at 0.0% after 3.9 days, defeated by a late tooling-gated start plus a 3-deep defect chain in a construction subsystem that had never been exercised before it was made load-bearing. The hard, expensive lesson (retro sp-lh69): **a gate mission that depends on an unexercised subsystem is a research project, not a deliverable, and cannot be finished inside a one-week universe.**

**RECOMMENDED POSTURE — for the Admiral to bless or override:**

- **PRIMARY, carries the era's grade: the proven income engine.** Contracts + manufacturing/trading were both validated, roughly co-equal earners in era 1 (48/52 split, ~150k/hr steady-state, 7-11x KPI). This is the safe, repeatable deliverable; the era succeeds or fails on it.
- **EARLY SHAKEDOWN, de-risking not a deliverable: exercise `construction start` against the real gate on day 1-2.** The dry-dock fixes — 73f3f08 (execution wiring), f7d644d (re-plan / poisoned-pipeline terminalize), 1fe4eff (depth-3 supply filter) — are all merged but UNEXERCISED against a real gate. Era 2's gate is the cheapest real test we will get. Point ONE earmarked hauler at it as soon as one can be spared without starving the earner (target day 1-2, not day 3), long enough to observe a single real OUTPUT: construction tasks created + gate material > 0 within one window. That validates the whole chain for every future era. If it works first-try, the gate graduates to the stretch below. If it hits a NEW (4th) defect, you learned it on day 1 instead of day 3.9: file ONCE, do NOT start another serial file→merge→deploy→restart chase (that is what sank era 1), surface `awaiting_human` with the full lineage, and fall the hauler back to income.
- **STRETCH, only if BOTH gates clear: finish the gate.** (1) The day-1 bill read shows the material bill is affordable inside the 50% capital guardrail; (2) the shakedown produced real material > 0 with no new defect. Absent either, do not commit the era to gate completion.

**Why this over the retro's two options** (pre-harden in a throwaway universe / scope the gate out): the fixes need a REAL gate to be exercised, era 2 has one, and an early bounded shakedown buys that validation without betting the era's grade on it. The Admiral may instead (a) commit fully to the gate — higher risk, retro-flagged as a research project in <7 days; or (b) scope the gate out entirely — safest, but the dry-dock fixes stay unexercised another era. Recommendation is the middle path.

**Law (d-85, non-negotiable): grade any subsystem "working" / "unblocked" only on first observable OUTPUT (material > 0), NEVER on a merge.** A merged fix does not repair the poisoned state a buggy binary left behind, and does not prove itself.

## KPI skeleton — universal now, map-specific after the first sweep

- **Steady-state $/hr:** baseline KPI ~21,900/hr (universal, carries across eras). Era 1 ran 7-11x it. Do NOT adopt era 1's ~150k absolute as a target — it was a function of that map's contract density and good spreads. Set the concrete steady-state target AFTER the first market sweep prices the map and the first hauler pool forms; until then the invariant is "decisively beat 21,900/hr and keep climbing."
- **Treasury guardrail:** deploy at most ~50% of treasury as capital at any time; keep ≥50% in reserve. Every hauler / factory / gate bet sizes against this cap.
- **Contract fulfillment:** hold ≥95% (era 1: 99% over 94 contracts).
- **Fleet utilization:** no ship idle > 60 min without a recorded reason.
- **Earner mechanic (why haulers help):** the binding constraint on the contract earner is CYCLE TIME, not contract supply — ~67% of a contract's wall-clock is travel (d-35/L48). The coordinator runs ONE contract at a time (L45), so extra haulers add positioning (shorter mean buy-leg via "closest ship"), not parallelism. Watch margin erosion (rising cargo cost vs payout) as the saturation signal.

## Bootstrap sequence — first ~24h

1. **Bring-up checks.** Prime; confirm the starting fleet (1 command ship + 1 probe) and container health. **Verify `RecoverOrphanedFixes` actually went live** — it was built pre-shutdown and goes live on THIS captain's first start; confirm startup resets any stranded `in_progress` fix reports (era-1 fixer-orphan class).
2. **Contract-first.** Contracts were the reliable bootstrap earner (48% of era-1 revenue) and need no capital. Accept + run the first contract on the command ship to move treasury before any bet.
3. **First market sweep BEFORE any capital bet.** The sequence is fixed: scout → wait for cached market data → then plan (purchase-planning fails fast without cached data). Reach **2-3 probes** early (buy 1-2 beyond the starting probe; ~22.5k each — the cheapest instrument you own). Budget ~30 market reads. **Locate the jump gate and read its bill on day 1** — that read is the decision gate for the stretch.
4. **First hauler + ALLOCATION POLICY, together.** Buy the 2nd contract hauler as soon as its cost fits inside the 50% capital budget — do NOT over-defer (era 1's recurring "right prediction, deferred decision" error). AT this purchase set the allocation policy up front: **earmark ships to roles — N contracts / M manufacturing / 1 gate — before manufacturing's discovery loop can greedily claim an idle hauler.** Era 1 let manufacturing starve the contract pool to ONE hauler and then tried to "buy around" it; build the policy, don't buy the symptom.
5. **Stand up manufacturing** once the sweep confirms a prefer-fabricate thesis (net-positive, on its earmarked hauler). Launch heavy workflows ONE AT A TIME — confirm health OK + container RUNNING before the next.
6. **Arm the stall tripwire from day one** (see risks): frozen ledger > 2h + negotiate-nil in the coordinator log → `ship refresh` every hauler.

## Priors to test — falsifiable, cheap, verify before betting capital

1. **Ship-component chain likely tops trading profit** (era 1: SHIP_PLATING / SHIP_PARTS / ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY led the board). Map-specific. Verify with the first full `market get` sweep (~30 reads) before committing capital. *Falsify: the sweep shows a different high-value chain — follow the data.*
2. **Contract-first bootstrap is still correct.** *Falsify: the first contract cycle's $/h is below the manufacturing first-collection rate — then reconsider the ordering.*
3. **Buy the 2nd contract hauler as soon as it clears the 50% guardrail.** The 2-ship pool compressed cycle time decisively (rate climbed 47k→92k/hr) and every hauler paid back within hours. *Falsify: a full day of a 2-ship pool doesn't lift $/h — the one-at-a-time idle penalty cancels the positioning gain.*
4. **Manufacturing is net-positive and does not sag contracts — IF its haulers are earmarked on standup.** *Falsify: watch contract $/h in the same window; if it drops, the streams are competing for the pool → fix allocation, not count.*
5. **Probe-per-2-3-markets coverage; probes are the cheapest instrument.** Stale market data blocked route validation and starved the manufacturing supply resolver in era 1. *Falsify: 2-3 probes don't improve data freshness enough to unblock routing — re-tour or add coverage.*
6. **Phantom cache desync WILL recur** (3 strains seen: cargo → position → negotiate-deadlock). Wire the tripwire day one. *Falsify: none expected — treat recurrence as the base case, not the exception.*
7. **"Not sold in-system" is time-stamped, not standing truth.** Re-sweep `market get` before locking any sourcing premise — era 1's gate materials appeared in-market two sessions after being recorded "unavailable," flipping a multi-hour fabrication into a direct buy. *Falsify: a re-sweep confirms persistent absence across several sessions.*
8. **The jump gate is probably the only exit again** (map-dependent; true in era 1 — no warp/jump hull was sold in-system). Locate it and read its bill on day one, then decide immediately whether construction is hardened enough to attempt inside the week. *Falsify: the shipyard sweep finds a warp/jump hull for sale — then exploration doesn't require the gate at all.*

## Standing risks — carried across eras

- **The cache-vs-server desync family (3 strains, WILL recur).** Phantom cargo → phantom position → phantom negotiate-deadlock. The era-1 headliner was an 18h fleet-wide income stall (~2-4M foregone): the coordinator error-loops `failed to negotiate: API returned nil result` while the daemon cache reads DOCKED but the server has moved the ship. **In-band remedy: `ship refresh` every hauler.** Tripwire: frozen ledger > 2h + negotiate-nil → refresh all haulers, from day one.
- **Orphan classes (restart / deploy state).** (a) **sp-tgp5 (P3, OPEN)** — worker-container orphan: a merge self-deploy restarts the daemon and kills in-flight worker containers; restart recovery re-instantiates only root/coordinator containers, so a mid-contract hauler is stranded and the coordinator loops "no ships available." Groom after bring-up. (b) **`RecoverOrphanedFixes`** — the fixer-orphan fix; goes live on this captain's first start. **Verify at bring-up** (step 1). (c) **sp-ciog (P3)** — restart recovery reaps tasks of transiently-inactive pipelines (the mechanism behind era-1 construction defect #2); relevant the moment you touch construction.
- **Unexercised fixes — trust NONE until each survives a real exercise (L42).** `ff22324` (contract-negotiate desync) is deployed but has never met a real desync — keep the `ship refresh` remedy armed regardless. `1fe4eff` (construction depth-3 supply filter — the live gate blocker at era-1 shutdown) is merged with tests but UNEXERCISED; the day-1/2 shakedown IS its first real test. Do not treat construction as reliable until material > 0.
- **Serial defect-chasing is a trap in a <7-day window.** Era 1 burned ~10 sessions on a tooling-gated start plus a 3-deep serial defect chain, each link a full merge/deploy/restart round-trip, and still finished at 0%. If the shakedown surfaces a new construction defect, file once and surface `awaiting_human` with the full lineage — do NOT start another file→merge→deploy→restart loop inside the week.

## Pointers

- **Retrospective:** bead **sp-lh69** (era torwind, decision-grade) — the source for every prior and risk above; read it in full at prime.
- **Prior strategy:** bead **sp-s2q** (CLOSED, era:torwind, demoted to retro input) — reuse its KPI skeleton and operational constraints; ignore its map-specific X1-PZ28 narrative.
- **Era-1 priors from telemetry:** `spacetraders history summary --era 1` and `spacetraders history goods --era 1` — re-read the income mix and top-goods board before setting era-2 market targets.
- **Open beads to groom after bring-up:** sp-tgp5 (worker-container orphan), sp-ciog (pipeline task-reap).

## Posture & maintenance

On first prime, append a **"Current posture (replace each session)"** block below this line, matching era-1 discipline: treasury, 24h $/hr vs KPI, guardrail cap, stream→ship assignments, and the single binding constraint. Revise this bead whenever actuals diverge from targets for 2+ consecutive sessions; keep it under ~150 lines; note the revision + reason in the captain log.

title: retro: era torwind
description:
# retro: era torwind

Decision-grade retrospective of the closed TORWIND era. Primes the era-2 captain and the Admiral. Honest, not ceremonial.

## Era facts

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Agent / faction | TORWIND / COSMIC |
| Universe resetDate | 2026-06-28 |
| Registered | 2026-07-02T21:47Z |
| Killed | 2026-07-05, server weekly reset (attributed, not a crash) |
| Closed | 2026-07-06 |
| Duration | 3.9 days |
| Final credits | 8,484,311 |
| Earn rate | steady-state peaked ~150k/hr; 24h instantaneous 122-242k/hr = ~7-11x the ~21,900/hr baseline KPI |
| Fleet | 1 command ship at start -> ~8-9 ships at end (1 command benched, 3 scouts/probes, 3 manufacturing, 1 contracts) |
| Income mix | 48.1% contract / 51.9% trading |
| Contracts | 94 accepted, 99% fulfillment |
| Next reset | 2026-07-12 (weekly cadence — this materially bounds mission scope; see postmortem) |

## What earned

Two engines, both validated, roughly co-equal by revenue.

- **Contracts (48.1%)** — the reliable bootstrap earner and the whole first ~30 sessions. Coordinator runs one contract at a time; the binding constraint is CYCLE TIME (travel/positioning is ~67% of wall-clock, d-35), not contract supply. A real multi-hauler pool compresses cycle time: rate climbed monotonically 26.6k -> 38.7k -> 47.3k -> 56.7k/hr across the first 2-ship experiment (d-37) and reached ~92.5k/hr by s70, ~129k/hr after the 2nd hauler (d-78).
- **Manufacturing/trading (51.9%)** — a `prefer-fabricate` production engine that stands up factories and sells output. Validated net-positive s84 (~+160k first collection, contracts did NOT sag — 24h KPI rose in the same window). Overtook contracts as the larger revenue share by era end.

**Top goods by trading profit** (manufacturing outputs, sold in-system):

| Good | Profit |
|---|---|
| SHIP_PLATING | 509,642 |
| SHIP_PARTS | 435,694 |
| ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY | 368,345 |
| FIREARMS | 290,018 |
| MICROPROCESSORS | 254,732 |

**As PRIORS, not facts** (this list is a function of *this* map's factory placement and demand — re-derive on the fresh map): shipyard-adjacent goods (plating, parts) topped the board, and one gate material (ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY) was independently a top-3 trade good — the same factories that fed the gate also earned. Expect a ship-component chain to be lucrative again, but verify with the first full market sweep before committing capital.

## What broke

**Reliability was mixed-but-recoverable.** Event highlights: 41 `container.crashed`, 14 `container.heartbeat_lost`, 60 `ship.idle`. Most crashes were absorbed by self-recovery (contracts RESUME after a restart frees their ships); the expensive ones were the stalls.

- **The 18-hour fleet-wide income stall (d-93)** — the single most expensive incident. Treasury frozen at 6,953,128 for 18h (2026-07-04 08:03Z onward); all 5 ships idle; only the contract coordinator alive, error-looping `failed to negotiate: API returned nil result` every ~60s. Root cause (full code trace): the negotiate path picks `availableShips[0]`, the server had the haulers IN_ORBIT/moved while the daemon cache said DOCKED, and the handler's dock-and-retry is a NO-OP when the local cache already reads DOCKED -> permanent loop, zero events emitted. **This is the third strain of the cache-vs-server desync family** (phantom cargo -> phantom position -> phantom negotiate-deadlock). In-band fix: `ship refresh` every hauler. A code fix merged + self-deployed (ff22324) but per L42 is UNEXERCISED against a real desync — treat as fixed only after it survives one. Foregone earnings ~2.2-4.4M.
- **Orphan classes (two, from restart/deploy events):**
  - *Fixer orphans* — a wind-down kill strands a fix report at `in_progress`; the fixer only picks `status:new`, so it never resumes. Fixed via `RecoverOrphanedFixes()` (supervisor resets stranded reports on startup). **Deployed pre-shutdown, NOT yet live — goes live on next captain start; verify it at era-2 bring-up.**
  - *Worker-container orphans* — every merge self-deploys = a daemon restart = in-flight worker containers die; restart recovery re-instantiates only root/coordinator containers, so a mid-contract hauler is orphaned (assignment active, no executor) and the coordinator loops "no ships available." **Still OPEN as sp-tgp5 (P3)** — same disease class as the fixer orphan; needs restart-state reconciliation generalized across both the fixer and the container runner.

## Mission postmortem: jump gate at 0.0%

The era's stated mission spine — the jump gate at X1-PZ28-I67 (FAB_MATS 0/1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 0/400; QUANTUM_STABILIZERS 1/1) — **ended at 0.0% material progress.** A full shipyard sweep (s90) confirmed no warp/jump hull is sold in-system: the completed gate is the ONLY exit from the system. It never moved for two compounding reasons.

**1. Late start (tooling-gated).** The gate wasn't even locatable until s67 — no waypoint-discovery verb existed before then. The s53 horizon plan (d-60) correctly diagnosed that the binding constraint on every non-contract horizon was TOOLING (a discovery verb), not idle capital. Capital was never scarce; the mission was blocked on missing verbs and, later, missing code.

**2. A 3-deep defect chain on a never-exercised subsystem.** Once launched (d-90, s85), the construction subsystem failed three distinct ways, each requiring a file -> merge -> deploy -> restart cycle:
   - **Defect #1 (d-90 close, s86):** construction was runtime-unwired — no activation path (every READY transition is type-filtered to FABRICATION/COLLECTION), no rescuer, and NO registered executor for `DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION`. The pipeline read "EXECUTING 0.0%" but no task could ever become READY. ~5h were burned on a wrong "hauler starvation" theory because per-task state is unobservable (no `tasks` verb) — the queue was graded by its inputs, not its mechanics.
   - **Defect #2 (d-91 close, s87):** fix merged same-day, but the old pipeline was an empty shell — its tasks had been reaped by restart recoveries, and `StartOrResume` returns immediately on any PLANNING/EXECUTING pipeline (never re-plans). A merged code fix does not repair the poisoned state a buggy binary left behind.
   - **Defect #3 (d-92 close / d-96, s91):** the re-plan fix worked (poisoned pipeline terminalized, site freed) — but depth-3 planning aborts because the market filter demands HIGH/ABUNDANT supply and ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY@D45 is only MODERATE, and the all-or-nothing loop discards the sourceable FAB_MATS leg too. Fix (d-96) is a one-line locator swap to the MODERATE-tolerant variant that already exists — **still unmerged at era shutdown; the gate died blocked here.** (It subsequently landed in dry dock: 1fe4eff rewired every planner call site to `FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority` with tests — merged, but UNEXERCISED against a real gate build.)
   - Meta-friction: the defect-#2 fix report was itself orphaned at `in_progress` by a wind-down kill; the **Admiral had to hand-deploy it mid-session** (f7d644d) — the same orphan class as sp-tgp5.

**Can a 1-week era carry a gate mission?** On this evidence, **no — not on an unexercised subsystem.** The gate consumed ~10 sessions of tooling-blocked waiting plus a 3-deep defect chain and still finished at 0% after 3.9 days, with each defect costing a full merge/deploy/restart round-trip. With resets now weekly (next 2026-07-12), a gate build that requires a never-run subsystem to work first-try is a research project, not a deliverable. **Carry-forward rule: pre-harden the construction subsystem in a throwaway universe BEFORE an era commits to a gate, or scope the era's mission to what a proven engine can finish in under 7 days.** Grade "unblocked" only on first observable OUTPUT (material > 0), never on a merge (d-85 line, now law).

## Fleet-balance lesson (d-95)

Manufacturing auto-claims idle light haulers with no exclusion flag (assignment-based mutual exclusion hides a claimed ship from other coordinators — mechanically correct). But it drifted the fleet: by s91 the contract pool was down to ONE hauler (TORWIND-5) because manufacturing had claimed TORWIND-3/4. d-95 recognized the drift and authorized another hauler — but was registered 02:40Z Jul 5 with its review window set AFTER the reset, so **it was never validated; the era died with the imbalance flagged and unfixed.** The response was "buy another hauler" — a treadmill that masks the imbalance instead of fixing it. Note that d-89/d-90's own falsify branch predicted exactly this: "if construction can't hold a hauler, the real blocker is coordinator hauler-reservation, not fleet count." It was prescient — allocation, not count, was the constraint.

**Do differently at the first hauler purchase:** decide an ALLOCATION POLICY up front — earmark ships to roles (N for contracts, M for manufacturing, 1 for the gate) — so whichever coordinator's discovery loop fires first can't greedily starve another stream. Build the policy, don't buy around the symptom.

## Priors to test early in era 2 (falsifiable, cheap)

1. **Ship-component chain likely tops trading profit** (SHIP_PLATING/SHIP_PARTS led torwind). Map-specific — verify with the first full `market get` sweep before betting capital. Cheap: ~30 reads.
2. **Contract-first bootstrap is still correct.** Contracts were the reliable early earner (48% of total). Stand up contracts before manufacturing. Falsify: if the first contract cycle's $/h is below the manufacturing first-collection rate, reconsider.
3. **Buy the 2nd contract hauler as soon as treasury clears the 50% guardrail** — don't over-defer. The 2-ship pool compressed cycle time decisively (47k -> 92k/hr) and every hauler buy validated within hours. Falsify: if a full day of a 2-ship pool doesn't lift $/h, the one-at-a-time idle penalty cancels the positioning gain.
4. **Manufacturing is net-positive and doesn't sag contracts** — but earmark its haulers on standup (see fleet-balance). Falsify: watch the contract $/h in the same window; if it drops, the streams are competing for the pool.
5. **Probe-per-2-3-markets coverage; probes are the cheapest instrument** (~22.5k each). Buy 2-3 early — stale market data blocked route validation and starved the mfg supply resolver in torwind.
6. **Phantom cache desync WILL recur** (3 strains seen). Wire the stall tripwire from day one: frozen ledger >2h + negotiate-nil in the coordinator log -> `ship refresh` all haulers.
7. **"Not sold in-system" is time-stamped, not standing truth** — re-sweep `market get` before locking any sourcing premise. In torwind, gate materials appeared in-market two sessions after being recorded "unavailable," flipping the plan from multi-hour fabrication to a direct buy.
8. **The jump gate is probably the only exit again** (map-dependent, but true in torwind). Locate it and read its bill on day one, then decide immediately whether the construction subsystem is hardened enough to attempt inside the week.

## Open threads carried across eras

- **sp-tgp5 (P3, OPEN)** — worker-container orphan: restarts kill in-flight contract workers, coordinator loops "no ships available." Generalize restart-state reconciliation across the fixer and the container runner. Groom after era-2 bring-up.
- **RecoverOrphanedFixes go-live verification** — built pre-shutdown, goes live on next captain start. Confirm at era-2 bring-up that startup actually resets stranded `in_progress` fix reports.
- **d-96 construction depth-3 supply filter** — the live gate blocker at era-end, unmerged at shutdown. Landed during dry dock (1fe4eff: planner rewired to the MODERATE-tolerant locator at all call sites, 13 tests; report status: merged). UNEXERCISED — era 2's first `construction start` is the real test.
- **Contract-negotiate desync fix (ff22324)** — deployed but UNEXERCISED against a real desync (L42); not proven until it survives one.
- **sp-ciog (P3 friction)** — restart recovery reaps tasks of transiently-inactive pipelines; the mechanism behind defect #2. Related to the re-plan/terminalize fix.

## Overall grade: B-minus — an A-grade earner around an F-grade mission

The income engine was a genuine success: 8.48M final, diversified 48/52 across two validated engines, 94 contracts at 99% fulfillment, steady-state ~150k/hr (7-11x KPI), and every capital bet repaid fast. The reliability record is mixed but honest — 41 crashes and an 18h stall that cost ~2-4M foregone, yet each incident was root-caused to a real defect and fixed in-band or by report. The mission was a clean miss: the jump gate, the era's declared spine and the system's only exit, ended at 0.0%, defeated by a late tooling-gated start and a 3-deep defect chain in a subsystem that had never been exercised before it was load-bearing. What redeems the era is its self-critique — the honest-holds discipline repeatedly caught its own "correct prediction, wrong decision" pattern (d-65, d-77) of over-deferring evidence-supported bets, and it graded itself on that standard without flinching. The single most important carryover is the hardest one: **a gate mission on an unexercised subsystem cannot be delivered inside a one-week universe. Pre-harden it or scope it out.**
notes:
Pre-rewrite originals of the 4 REWRITE-class memories (preserved before any bd remember runs, mirroring the runbook's RETIRE preservation discipline):

== l47-d-34-d-39-d-87-phantom (pre-rewrite original, preserved verbatim)
L47 [d-34,d-39,d-87] — Phantom cache (cargo/position/role) RECURS after each contract; the in-band fix is `ship refresh --ship <sym>` (force GET /my/ships), now allowlisted (s32). First move on ANY confirmed phantom — don't restart a healthy daemon to clear it. It doesn't only break sell/deliver: a phantom FOREIGN-CARGO hold silently BENCHES a hauler from the coordinator's eligible pool (looks half-full → not claimable). A HAULER idle across sessions with stale partial cargo is a refresh candidate, not a lost ship (s81: TORWIND-3 cached 44/80 IRON_ORE, server 0/80). `ship refresh` ALONE re-arms it — the contract coordinator re-discovers idle light haulers IN-LOOP every contract-accept, so a phantom-cleared EXISTING-role hauler rejoins the eligible pool with NO container restart (s82/d-87: TORWIND-3 ran 3 contracts post-refresh). The L50 restart caveat is specific to ROLE-assignment visibility, not idle-hauler discovery.

== l56-d-88-to-measure-manufacturing-trading-standalone (pre-rewrite original, preserved verbatim)
L56 [d-88] — To measure manufacturing/trading standalone $/h: `ledger list --type SELL_CARGO` isolates it CLEANLY — contracts emit CONTRACT_FULFILLED and NEVER SELL_CARGO, so gross trading revenue = sum(SELL_CARGO) with zero hand-pairing (only the BUY side stays muddied, L45). TWO gotchas: (a) the ledger displays in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC — align a sell to its task by the exact 3h offset (sell 21:53:20 local == task-complete 00:53:20 UTC); (b) a factory's FIRST collection dumps ACCUMULATED inventory, so the first COLLECT_SELL task's rate OVERSTATES steady state (task eebee24b: +229,740 rev / ~68,856 buy = ~+160k net in 4min — do not annualize one draw, L41).

== l58-d-90-s86-not-sold-in-system (pre-rewrite original, preserved verbatim)
L58 [d-90,s86] — "Not sold in-system" is a TIME-STAMPED observation, not standing truth: factory markets wake their export side once anyone (including YOUR OWN pipelines) feeds their imports. FAB_MATS appeared at F56 (532, ABUNDANT) and ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY at D45 (1,900, MODERATE) two sessions after "unavailable" was recorded from an I67/A1/A2 spot-check — flipping the gate plan from a multi-hour fabrication campaign to a ~1.6M direct buy. Re-sweep `market get` across the system before locking any plan premise (depth selection, "must fabricate", sourcing) to a stale availability claim; the sweep costs ~30 reads, the wrong premise costs sessions.

== l7-seed-accept-a-marginal-or-slightly-negative (pre-rewrite original, preserved verbatim)
L7 [seed] — Accept a marginal or slightly-negative contract when capital allows: it builds reputation and unlocks the next, potentially lucrative, contract. title: Surveyor: automatic meta-improvement loop for both engines (LLM + gobot)
description:
Admiral requirement (2026-07-06): meta-improvement must be AUTOMATIC; harbormaster is manual-only and the wrong home. Design:

(1) SURVEYOR - new standing city agent (city/agents/surveyor, opus), the marine-surveyor of the operation: review-only, never edits anything. Woken by the WATCHKEEPER on a cadence (config captain.meta_review_days=7, or every N captain sessions - whichever first). Reads: engine report + decision beads (outcome-completion sample), captain_events ack latency, consult answer latency, engine-labeled friction, session transcripts token costs, template-vs-practice drift. OUTPUT: engine-labeled bug/feature beads with evidence into the shipwright queue + one-screen digest mail to Admiral. Structural independence: separate session+template from the captain (self-review drift blindness was why old metareview.go existed; it was deleted at cutover with no true replacement - this is the successor, OUTSIDE the thing it reviews).

(2) ENGINE REPORT - small read-only verb (spacetraders engine report [--json]): wakes/day, tokens/wake (transcript parse - dashboard token_stats logic exists), event ack latency, decisions opened vs closed-with-outcome, consults answered vs pending, rollover count. Gives the surveyor numbers instead of vibes.

(3) WATCHKEEPER wiring - nudge surveyor on cadence; DISABLED kill switch structurally silences it like everything else. Tier 3 (watchkeeper touch).

(4) LABEL CONVENTION - captain/crew templates gain one line: engine-friction files with label 'engine' (distinct from fleet friction). Tier 3 (template edit).

(5) LOOP CLOSURE - surveyor beads flow the EXISTING tiers: Tier 1/2 (gobot-side: detectors, verbs, report improvements) fully automatic behind the gate; Tier 3 (templates/watchkeeper/gate) queues for Admiral sign-off. Deliberate ceiling: the engine never gets autonomy over its own cage - automatic UP TO the approval line. Era retrospective (archive spec section 4.4) remains the deep per-universe review; surveyor is the weekly shallow loop.

BUILD: blocked until archive-reset workflow lands (file collision: internal/captain + config). Admiral order = Tier-3 sign-off; recommend building on fresh pool.

Name rationale: marine surveyor - inspects vessels for seaworthiness, reports to owners, repairs are the shipwright's job. Exactly the division of labor.

Track A COMMITTED: surveyor template (fable-reviewed), captain report v1, all 6 templates audited+fixed by fable (shipwright DISABLED gap closed, harbormaster drift healed, cross-template protocol consistency enforced, renders verified). title: Cross-universe game archive: preserve reset universes as a learnable history corpus
description:
Admiral requirement (2026-07-06): on universe reset, do NOT just wipe - ARCHIVE each universe's history so cross-era data improves design + play. Reframes scout feature #8 from wipe-hygiene to archive-then-reset.

WHAT'S CROSS-UNIVERSE (archive + query): transactions/P&L, contract economics (which goods/types paid), market_price_history (GOOD-level volatility/supply behaviour repeats across universes even as maps differ - cf lesson l18 fuel-is-volatile), manufacturing chain profitability, captain_events. Qualitative half (decisions/lessons/strategy) ALREADY cross-universe + persistent in the sp- beads ledger post-bridge - it just accumulates eras.
WHAT'S PER-UNIVERSE (wipe): map (systems/waypoints/coords), ship instances, the gate, active operations/pipelines.

RECOMMENDED SHAPE: era-stamped archive tables (or schema) populated from live rows on reset BEFORE truncating the map/fleet tables; era key = universe/agent/faction + reset timestamp. Plus a read verb (spacetraders history goods --good X / history contracts) so the captain + Trade-Analyst prime the new universe with priors at cold-start.
ALTERNATIVES: full pg_dump per era (simple backup, NOT queryable-live for priming); universe_id column on live tables never-deleted (unbounded + poison-risk if a filter is forgotten - rejected).
PREREQUISITE to st-wm7 new-universe bring-up: archive must run before any live-table reset. Consumers: design-time cross-era analysis (Admiral), play-time priors (captain/analysts at cold-start).
notes:
BEADS-LAYER DESIGN (corrects the earlier 'beads are cross-universe for free' framing - they're a MIX):

UNIVERSE-INDEPENDENT (persist untouched across resets, no era): code-improvement features (scout backlog), bugs, the Shipwright queue, and UNIVERSAL lessons ('fuel is volatile', 'frozen ledger is the alarm'). These are about the BOT, not the game state.

UNIVERSE-SCOPED (era-label + archive on reset): tactical decisions (nearly all - they name specific ships/waypoints/the gate), universe-specific lessons ('D45 is the only ADV_CIRCUITRY exporter'), and the living strategy bead. Design: label era:<name>; on reset bulk-close the OPEN ones (they stay queryable as history: bd list -l era:torwind --status closed), start a fresh era. Retrospective across closed eras seeds the new universe's fresh strategy + priors.

SHARPEST RISK - MEMORIES: bd remember memories are AUTO-PRIMED into every session. A universe-specific memory would prime a fresh captain with FALSE priors. Reset needs a memory-review gate: keep universal, retire/era-tag universe-specific.

IMMEDIATE CONSEQUENCE (state I just created): the migration imported 216 TORWIND (universe-1) decisions + 61 lessons into the live sp- ledger, currently UN-tagged. Before st-wm7 brings up universe-2, these must be era:torwind-tagged and the open decisions closed, and the strategy bead sp-s2q demoted to a retrospective seed - else the universe-2 captain reads universe-1 tactics as current. The reset process, not the migration, does this.
FULL DESIGN SPEC written + committed: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-cross-universe-archive-reset-design.md (520 lines, reviewed by harbormaster - classification + runbook read in full, sound). This is the design of record. Shipwright Tier 3 (schema + destructive verb + Watchkeeper + templates -> Admiral sign-off), 7-step gated build order in the spec. AutoMigrate structural fix (AllModels registry) is step 1.
SPEC AMENDED to rev 2 per Admiral challenge: in-place player-partitioned history (player_id IS the era key; verified 13/17 models carry it). No archive schema/copying; eras registry + waypoints.era_id only; universe close+scrub replace archive/verify/truncate; runbook 9 phases. Build order in spec updated (step 1 AllModels already shipped). Still Tier 3, still awaiting Admiral build sign-off.
Admiral: BUILD WITH WORKFLOW (2026-07-06) = Tier-3 sign-off on rev 2. Launching: foundations (eras+era_id+GetServerStatus+status verb, opus) -> parallel lanes close/scrub (opus), history verbs+CI guard (sonnet/opus), watchkeeper reset detector (opus craft, FABLE review - kill-switch rail), register --new (opus), runbook+era-close helper (sonnet) -> full gate.

# Standing strategy

## Standing Admiral directives
**#1 FABRICATION PATH (s71, Admiral order #1).** The jump-gate bill (FAB_MATS 1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 400 at
X1-PZ28-I67) and the manufacturing income stream run on the SAME engine: `operations start --system X1-PZ28
--manufacturing` (discovers high-demand goods, prefer-fabricate resolver) + targeted `goods produce <GOOD> --system
X1-PZ28`. PREMISE CORRECTED s86 (L58): both goods are NOW sold in-system — our own factories woke the exports
(FAB_MATS @F56 532 ABUNDANT, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY @D45 1,900 MODERATE) → depth-3 buy-final IS available, bill ≈1.6–2M.
GAS OPS EVALUATED s86 (Admiral ask): IMMATERIAL — 0% of fabrication inputs are siphonable (gases only enter recipes
via EXPLOSIVES, never reached; resolver buys ore/sand/crystal leaves) and gases cost 23–28 at C42 anyway. CLOSED;
plumbing (STORAGE_ACQUIRE_DELIVER, cost-0) exists if a gas-consuming chain ever matters. Manufacturing auto-claims idle light
haulers with NO exclusion flag, BUT ship ASSIGNMENTS already give mutual exclusion (a claim writes
`assignment_status='active'` → hidden from every other coordinator), so a manufacturing/construction hauler is
auto-excluded from contracts with NO flag needed (reservation flag REJECTED as phantom, reverted 6fee4f1).
X1-PZ28 has a real prefer-fabricate thesis (cheap ores J70/B7 → SCARCE high-value finished goods A1) but sell
volumes 6–20 SCARCE/WEAK cap standalone $/h (L13). `goods produce` has NO --dry-run (friction).

## KPI targets
- **Credits/hour: baseline ~21,900/hr; ACTUAL ~216k/hr (~9.9× KPI, s86), climbing since manufacturing went live.** The binding
  constraint on the EARNER is CYCLE TIME, not contract supply (d-35/L48): contract EXECUTION = ~67% of wall-clock
  is travel; cadence is endogenous (coordinator negotiates next contract on fulfillment). A 2nd hauler cut mean
  buy-leg distance via the "select closest ship" balancer → more cycles/hour. Coordinator runs ONE contract at a
  time (L45) — extra haulers add positioning, not parallelism. NET per mega-contract ~+174k at ~67–73% margin
  (L45). Watch margin erosion (rising cargo cost vs payout) as the saturation signal (L13).
- Fleet utilization: no ship idle > 60 min without a recorded reason.

## Horizon plan (Admiral challenge, s53 d-60) — mission beyond credits/hour
**Jump gate is the mission spine; idle capital is NOT the constraint — TOOLING/DEFECTS are.** Treasury ~3.27M
compounding autonomously; capital is not scarce. Ranked portfolio (cost-to-unblock × mission value):
- **#1 JUMP GATE — the progression spine.** Located X1-PZ28-I67 (s67). Unlocks the interstellar network. Bill:
  FAB_MATS 0/1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 0/400 (QUANTUM_STABILIZERS 1/1 done), 0.0% built. Both materials now BUYABLE
  in-system (L58, ≈1.6–2M total) — blocked ONLY by the s86 construction-execution bug (report filed, d-91).
- **#2 EXPLORATION — unlocks #1's payoff.** Gated on jump capability. s90: full shipyard sweep confirms NO warp/jump
  hull is sold in-system — the completed gate is the ONLY route. Recon-first with a cheap probe, never a capital ship blind.
- **#3 MANUFACTURING / TRADING — EXECUTABLE, defect-gated.** The engine is built + allowlisted parallel income.
  Real gates now: (a) the d-86 `sequence_number` migration (below); (b) sell-side volume 6–20 SCARCE/WEAK caps
  standalone $/h; (c) validation before any capital buy (L16).
- **#4 FLEET — derived, demand-pulled.** Buy only against a validated, unblocked mission — NOT because treasury is
  big (L16). Gate-build + manufacturing each want a dedicated hauler (assignment-excluded, no flag needed).

### MANUFACTURING EXPERIMENT (d-65/d-88) — MEASURED s84: NET-POSITIVE, contracts unharmed → SCALE TRIGGER FIRED
- **Status:** VALIDATED NET-POSITIVE. s84 measured the window (L56 method: `ledger list --type SELL_CARGO` isolates
  trading revenue cleanly — contracts never emit SELL_CARGO). Result: the ONLY 5 SELL_CARGO rows in the 567-row
  ledger are all task eebee24b = **+229,740 gross vs ~68,856 buy = ~+160k net in ~4min**. CAVEAT: one task, and a
  factory's FIRST collection dumps accumulated inventory → this rate OVERSTATES steady state (L41/L56); the per-task
  rate will fall once initial stock clears, but the collection STREAM is continuous. Contracts did NOT sag:
  CONTRACT_FULFILLED +171,892 at 00:49Z + 24h KPI ROSE to +142,160/hr (from +131,858). Coordinator claims ONLY
  TORWIND-4 (released between tasks); TORWIND-3 stayed contract-locked — no over-claim.
- **Stop path (d-65 GUARD, corrected L53):** `operations stop --manufacturing` DOES NOT WORK (registry doesn't track
  it) — halt via `container stop parallel_manufacturing-X1-PZ28-f388df4b`. Observe via `container logs`.
- **SCALE TRIGGER — FIRED s84 (d-89):** both conditions met (net-positive + no contract sag) → bought 1 dedicated
  LIGHT_HAULER (`shipyard purchase --ship TORWIND-1 --type SHIP_LIGHT_HAULER --budget 300000`, container
  batch_purchase_ships-TORWIND-1-2e22cde5 in-flight, lands ~next session). PURPOSE: free a hauler to run gate
  CONSTRUCTION in parallel without stalling contracts/manufacturing.
- **RESOLVED s85 (d-90, L57):** `construction start` spawns NO coordinator — the MANUFACTURING coordinator executes
  construction via ONE unified task queue, adopting pipelines only at startup recovery (order: construction start
  FIRST → then bounce operations --manufacturing).
- **RESOLVED s86 (d-90 closed FAILED, L59): the starvation theory was ALSO wrong — it's a BUG.** Construction tasks
  can never run on the current binary: no activation path (all PENDING→READY paths are type-filtered to
  FABRICATION/COLLECTION), no rescuer case, and NO registered executor for DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION (main.go:499-508).
  Queue priority actually FAVORS construction (75>50>10) — the task simply never becomes READY. Report filed:
  reports/bugs/2026-07-04-construction-pipeline-tasks-never-execute.md (kind:fix). Pipeline 6c02cbe3 reads
  "EXECUTING 0.0%" and will stay there until the fix merges + daemon restarts.

### Sequencing (dependency-ordered)
1. DONE (s67): waypoint-discovery verb shipped; jump gate located (X1-PZ28-I67); bill read. Re-read the bill each
   session (external agents may also supply it; QUANTUM_STABILIZERS already went 1/1).
2. DONE (s83): d-86 `sequence_number` migration merged (commit 5095c78) + daemon restarted → column self-healed;
   `operations start --manufacturing` now persists pipelines + claims a hauler with no 42703 (d-88).
3. DONE (s84): d-65 experiment MEASURED — manufacturing NET-positive (~+160k first task, L56), contracts unharmed
   (KPI rose to +142k/hr). SCALE TRIGGER fired: d-89 bought 1 dedicated LIGHT_HAULER (in-flight).
4. DONE (s85): d-89 hauler landed (TORWIND-5, refreshed, role HAULER) + closed. Gate build LAUNCHED (d-90): learned
   the execution model (L57 — construction start is inert until the mfg coordinator is bounced to adopt it), bounced
   the coordinator → construction pipeline 6c02cbe3 EXECUTING at `--max-workers 2`. Gate still 0.0% material (depth-1
   fabrication chain is multi-hour).
5. DONE (s86): gate verified STILL 0.0% after 5h → root-caused as a BUG (not starvation, L59): construction subsystem
   is runtime-unwired (no activation, no executor). Report filed (kind:fix), d-90 closed failed, d-91 opened. Gas ops
   evaluated for the Admiral: IMMATERIAL (0% of input cost; numbers in s86 log). Discovered depth-3 is now viable (L58).
6. DONE (s87): d-91 report merged in <3h (73f3f08) AND self-deployed (daemon restarted 12s post-merge; wiring
   confirmed live in code). But depth-3 start hit a SECOND defect: resume returns the s85 pipeline as an EMPTY SHELL
   (tasks reaped by restart recoveries; StartOrResume never re-plans, nothing terminalizes a 0-task pipeline, L60).
   d-91 closed failed; second report filed; d-92 opened.
7. DONE (s91): d-92's orphaned pipeline run (wind-down killed it mid-merge; fixer has NO orphan recovery — picks only
   status:new) was hand-deployed by the ADMIRAL mid-session (f7d644d + daemon restart 03:02:39Z + supervisor orphan
   recovery 16f2e66). Exercised immediately: re-plan WORKS — poisoned 6c02cbe3 terminalized FAILED, site freed. But
   depth-3 planning hit defect #3 (L62 chain): market filter demands HIGH/ABUNDANT, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY@D45 is
   MODERATE, all-or-nothing loop discards the ABUNDANT FAB_MATS leg too. Report filed
   (2026-07-05-construction-depth3-supply-filter-too-strict: swap to the existing MODERATE+ locator), d-92 closed
   worked, d-96 opened.
8. IN PROGRESS (d-96, next sessions): (a) WATCH the supply-filter report → merged (fix is DAEMON-side: planner runs
   in the daemon, needs the post-merge restart). (b) Then `construction start X1-PZ28-I67 --depth 3` → verify tasks
   for BOTH materials, then material > 0 within one window; bill re-priced s91 ≈1.59M (FAB_MATS 1600@520 F56 +
   ADV_CIRCUITRY 400@1893 D45), ceiling 2.2M. (c) A FOURTH defect → stop filing serially, awaiting_human with the
   full 4-report chain. (d) Then: gate operational → cheap probe recon of nearest connected system.

## Current posture (REPLACE each session — do not append)
**s91 (2026-07-05): the gate's defect CHAIN advanced one link (L62). The d-92 fix — orphaned at in_progress by the
wind-down kill (fixer picks only status:new; stale branch+worktree left) — was hand-deployed by the ADMIRAL
mid-session (f7d644d, daemon restart 03:02:39Z, supervisor orphan-recovery 16f2e66). Exercised at once: poisoned
pipeline 6c02cbe3 terminalized, site FREED. Defect #3 found + filed same session: depth-3 planning demands
HIGH/ABUNDANT supply, ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY@D45 is MODERATE → whole plan aborts (d-96).**
Treasury **~7.70M**, 24h delta +153,323/hr (~7× KPI). Guardrail ≤50% ≈ **~3.85M cap**. Health ✓ (9 containers).
Streams post-deploy-restart: contracts = TORWIND-5; manufacturing = TORWIND-3/4/8; scouts = TORWIND-2/6/7 (d-94).
TORWIND-1 parked DOCKED at H64 (benched command ship, standing reason). All coordinators recovered post-restart.
Shipyard catalog (s90) stands: NO warp/jump hull in-system — the gate is the ONLY path off this system.
Gate: FAB_MATS 0/1600 + ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY 0/400; site has NO active pipeline (poison cleared); bill re-priced
≈1.59M; blocked ONLY by the d-96 supply-filter report (status: new).
**BINDING CONSTRAINT: the d-96 supply-filter fix** — a locator swap (MODERATE+ variant already exists in code).
Income, capital, fleet all healthy; nothing else blocks the mission spine.
**NEXT SESSION MUST:** (a) d-96: check report status → if merged (fix is daemon-side, restart included in pipeline
deploy): `construction start X1-PZ28-I67 --depth 3` → verify tasks for BOTH materials, then material > 0; ceiling
2.2M; a 4th defect → awaiting_human with the full chain. (b) d-95 review (03:00Z Jul 6): mfg SELL_CARGO throughput
vs the 2-worker baseline; contract pool at ONE hauler — if 24h $/h sags <130k, weigh a 5th hauler vs rebalancing.
(c) d-94 review (02:00Z Jul 6): 3-probe coverage improving data freshness. (d) POST-DEPLOY CHECKLIST standing (L61):
after ANY merged report → container list + verify every mid-workflow ship has an executor; expect ~5-10min gap.
(e) STALL TRIPWIRE standing: frozen ledger >2h + negotiate-nil in coordinator log → ship refresh all haulers
(d-93 fix deployed but unexercised against a real desync).

## Operational constraints
- **Launch heavy workflows ONE AT A TIME** (L25). Concurrent heavy launches transiently hung the daemon socket.
  Launch → confirm `health` ok + container RUNNING → then launch the next.
- **Sequence: scout → wait for market data → batch-contract** (L21/L27). Purchase-planning fails fast without
  cached market data.

## Degraded capabilities (current)
- **Gate construction: BLOCKED on defect #3 of the chain (s91, L62) — depth-3 planning aborts because the market
  filter (FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply) accepts only HIGH/ABUNDANT supply and ADVANCED_CIRCUITRY@D45 is MODERATE;
  the all-or-nothing materials loop discards the sourceable FAB_MATS leg too.** Report filed s91
  (2026-07-05-construction-depth3-supply-filter-too-strict, d-96). RESOLVED upstream: execution wiring live
  (73f3f08); poisoned-pipeline trap FIXED + EXERCISED (f7d644d, s91 — stale empty pipelines are terminalized and
  re-planned; site currently has NO active pipeline). Depth 2 is NOT a workaround (same filter on every intermediate
  input). Manufacturing itself HEALTHY (TORWIND-3/4/8). Standing gotchas: (a) L57 bounce is only for ADOPTING a new
  pipeline — activation polls the DB; (b) observability: use container verbs, not `operations status` (L53); no
  task-level visibility, no `construction stop`, no cost dry-run (s84/s86/s87 friction).
- **Contract negotiation: fix MERGED + DEPLOYED (s89, ff22324) but UNEXERCISED against a real desync** — per L42,
  treat as fixed only after it survives one; until then the L47/L61 remedy stands (`ship refresh` every hauler on
  negotiate-nil loops).
- **Merge self-deploys KILL in-flight worker containers (s89, code-traced).** Restart recovery re-instantiates only
  root/coordinator containers (daemon_server.go:635-641 skips workers → FAILED); `ReleaseAllActive` at startup frees
  the ships and accepted contracts RESUME, so the net cost is a ~5-10min gap + pool reshuffle. Contracts have NO
  stale-worker rescuer (mfg does, container_repository.go:393-447) — a failed sweep = permanently wedged ship, no
  CLI recovery. Post-deploy checklist in Current posture.
- **Coordinators do NOT survive external stops (s88).** The mfg coordinator was stopped externally 07:22:41Z Jul 4
  (attribution unknown) and its max_iterations=1 recovery record meant no relaunch; scout tours end without relaunch
  too. After ANY suspected daemon event: `container list` and relaunch operations --manufacturing + scout-all-markets.
- **Contract visibility: NONE.** No `contract list`; contract state is observable only via container logs.
  Deadlines unobservable — track accepted contracts by hand.
- **Daemon restart: NOT a Captain verb.** On a hang, rely on self-recovery + `tools/wait-daemon.sh` /
  `tools/restart-daemon.sh` (guardrails: restart once per session, only after SOCKET-DEAD).
- (Resolved and archived to the log: phantom-cargo/position desync, ship-sell nil-panic, socket-hang,
  treasury-readout garbage, d-83 daemon restart-loop. Re-verify by exercising, not status alone — L39/L42.)

## Revision protocol
Revise this file at any heartbeat where actuals diverge from targets for 2+ consecutive sessions. Replace Current
posture every session; keep the file under ~150 lines. Note the revision + reason in captain-log.md.

No probe1/probe2 siblings found open or closed. If this was a watchkeeper/requeue test artifact, close it; if real work, it needs a description. Flagged to the Admiral in today's port report.

title: player register --new prints FULL API token unmasked (player_register_new.go:97)
description: Flagged during sp-byza (token masking for 'player info'). Separate exposure: 'spacetraders player register --new' prints the entire JWT unmasked at player_register_new.go:97 (fmt.Fprintf(out, "  Token:  %s\n", player.Token)). Lower frequency than 'player info' (register runs only at era reset, not routinely), so it was outside byza's scope. Apply the SAME maskToken(player.Token, showToken) helper (now in player.go) + a --show-token flag on the register command. One-time era-reset commands still leak the full token into the setup transcript. Reuse the existing maskToken helper — do not duplicate it.

title: trade-route as a daemon container (single-writer safe) — promote the CLI runner
description: sp-s7c2 Part-2 follow-up (2026-07-07). The trade-route coordinator (application/trading/commands/run_trade_route_coordinator.go, RunTradeRouteCoordinatorHandler) is CLI-driven today = a DUAL-WRITER with the daemon on ship state (safe only on genuinely idle-gap hulls; refuses non-idle claims but has a TOCTOU if the daemon grabs the hull mid-circuit). It's already daemon-grade (Handle matches common.RequestHandler, same driven ports as the fabrication coordinators). Promote it to a daemon CONTAINER kind for single-writer safety: new container kind + ContainerSpec + StartTradeRoute RPC + runtime dispatch + proto regen. Then the captain can run disciplined arbitrage circuits on idle-gap capacity WITHOUT the dual-writer race. Needs daemon runtime wiring + proto (couldn't be validated under no-live-daemon). Relatedly: the CLI runner should also refuse to claim a hull the daemon is actively flying, not just !IsIdle at claim.

title: investigate possible mfg ledger double-count: records via ledger_recorder AND cargo commands
description: sc6u deviation-2 flag (2026-07-07): manufacturing appears to record transactions via BOTH internal/application/manufacturing/.../ledger_recorder.go AND (separately) the cargo commands path. If both fire for the same buy/sell, the ledger double-counts that transaction. sc6u's single-writer fix makes each RECORD's balance authoritative but does not dedup two records of the same event. Investigate: trace one mfg buy end-to-end, confirm whether both paths record it; if so, pick ONE canonical recorder. Out of sc6u scope; treasury-accuracy relevant.

title: audit/guard: every coordinator constructor must nil-substitute its clock (bt6o was the lone omission)
description: sp-bt6o root cause (2026-07-07): the goods-factory constructor lacked the 'if clock==nil { clock=shared.NewRealClock() }' guard that main.go relies on (it passes nil expecting substitution). Every OTHER coordinator has it; the factory's omission SIGSEGV'd the whole daemon once vmrj made its claim path reachable. Durable fix (tvcm-style): a test that asserts every handler constructed with a nil clock does not nil-deref clock.Now() on its hot paths — OR a lint that flags a constructor storing a clock param without the nil-substitute. Prevents the whole class recurring when a new coordinator is added.

Config = Tier-3-adjacent; needs sign-off + a config edit, not touched by the templates-only 4xs1.

title: 42703 column-level schema-drift gate: GORM model fields vs migration columns
description: sp-tvcm follow-up (fix agent flag, 2026-07-07): tvcm built the 23514 ENUM CHECK-constraint drift gate (merged 55025f4). The bead's literal headline also named a 42703 COLUMN-level gate — a genuinely larger, different check: reflect GORM struct fields -> column names, compare against columns created/altered by migrations (CREATE TABLE + ALTER ADD COLUMN across all up.sql), fail when a model persists a column no migration defines (undefined_column at write). Same defect CLASS as sp-8ind but the column axis, not the enum axis. Also worth folding: trade_type (mig 019) + pipeline_type (mig 020) are enum-valued columns with NO CHECK constraint — either add constraints (then tvcm's gate covers them) or accept them as unconstrained. Not built by tvcm; needs GORM reflection over the model registry.

title: Config-driven crash-loop tuning: CaptainConfig fields for window/threshold
description: Follow-up to sp-no9i. detectCrashLoops (gobot/internal/captain/detectors.go) is wired live from in-package defaults (defaultCrashLoopWindow=30m, defaultCrashLoopThreshold=3) because CaptainConfig was out of sp-no9i's scope. Add CrashLoopWindowMinutes + CrashLoopThreshold to config.CaptainConfig (internal/infrastructure/config/captain.go) + defaults.go, and populate dcfg in supervisor.go from them (mirroring StreamDownMinutes/ExpectedStreams) so operators can tune without a rebuild. Keep the in-package consts as the fallback default.

title: Deploy/env hardening: plist generated from repo template (BD_REAL can't be dropped) + build-stamp startup banners with deploy assertion
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. Two deploy/env hardenings that retire recurring hazard classes: (a) BD_REAL plist fragility — ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist must carry BD_REAL=<real bd path> or ALL watchkeeper gc/bd calls exit 127 (the historical whole-fleet outage; memory captain-supervisor-plist-*). Machine-local + hand-maintained = will be dropped on the next regen. FIX: generate the plist from an in-repo template via a make target (make install-launchd or similar) that always includes BD_REAL + correct paths; hand-editing stops being load-bearing. (b) stale-binary class (L42, and the near-miss in the 2026-07-07 survey): a merged fix is invisible if the running binary predates it. FIX: embed a build stamp (git sha + build time) in watchkeeper + daemon startup banners, and make deploy targets assert the freshly-built stamp appears in the live process log after restart. TEST: plist regen from clean checkout carries BD_REAL; make deploy fails loudly if the old binary is still running. RETIRES captain-supervisor-plist-* (fact already merging into the watchkeeper memory via curation) and L42's stale-binary clause on landing.
close_reason: merged 87bac4b. TWO deliverables: (1) COMMITTED launchd templates (deploy/launchd/*.plist.template + render-launchd.sh, paths parameterized) — durable source of truth so a plist regen can NEVER again drop BD_REAL/watchkeeper/ExitTimeOut (the sk68 + t0f6 hand-fixes are now permanent). Agent found the 4 previously-committed plists were ALL STALE (wrong ~/projects path, bin/captain not watchkeeper, no BD_REAL) and replaced them. (2) BUILD-STAMP: -ldflags -X embeds commit+buildtime; watchkeeper/daemon print it at startup, 'spacetraders version'/--version expose it — VERIFIED live (version prints 87bac4b + timestamp). Makefile LDFLAGS applied to all 3 build targets with safe 'unknown'/'dev' defaults; my normal build-daemon/build-cli/build-watchkeeper now stamp automatically. DEPLOY POSTURE: build stamps take effect on each binary's NEXT rebuild (daemon at hs2j restart via 'make assert-live-stamp' bonus verification; watchkeeper next kickstart; CLI done). install-launchd/deploy targets are AVAILABLE but NOT auto-run — re-bootstrapping live units mid-era-mission is disruptive and unnecessary (live units already work); left for a deliberate maintenance window. FLAG on that future apply: install-launchd would ADD ExitTimeOut=45 to the live captain unit (currently has none — harmless drain ceiling, but a conscious change). 5 buildinfo tests TDD RED-proven, gates green. No memory retired (infra).

title: construction start creates a pipeline nothing adopts until a manual coordinator bounce (check sp-mvcr overlap first)
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L57: 'construction start' creates an INERT pipeline (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION, status PLANNING) but spawns NO coordinator, and the manufacturing coordinator adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (no re-poll in the main loop) — required folklore is 'construction start FIRST, then bounce operations --manufacturing'. FIX: make adoption not require a manual coordinator bounce (re-poll for PLANNING pipelines in the coordinator main loop, or have construction start trigger adoption). FIRST CHECK OVERLAP with sp-mvcr (commit acb98f9 guarded DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION dispatchability — that addressed executor/activation, possibly not adoption re-poll); close as duplicate if fully covered, else fix the residual. RETIRES L57's ordering clause on landing.
close_reason: merged 4caab94. Coordinator now adopts PLANNING/unregistered construction pipelines in the main loop (AdoptUnregisteredConstructionPipelines, called before rescue so a just-Started pipeline's delivery tasks dispatch same-cycle) — no manual 'bounce operations' bounce. NOT an mvcr dup (confirmed: mvcr guarded dispatchability/activation, never adoption re-poll). Regression test 133 lines (runtime-created pipeline adopted within a poll cycle, no restart). Gate green. DEPLOY: batched with other daemon fixes this wave — not yet restarted; retires L57 ordering-clause folklore ('construction start FIRST then bounce') on deploy. Flag to sp-4ek3 curation.

title: operations status/stop blind to running manufacturing coordinators — silently report nothing while it runs
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L53 (generalized s83): 'operations status' and 'operations stop --manufacturing' do NOT track a manufacturing operation even when normally launched — status prints 'No active manufacturing operations' while the coordinator RUNS under 'Other Containers', and stop returns 'No matching operations'. Folklore workaround: always observe/halt via 'container logs'/'container stop <parallel_manufacturing-… id>'. FIX: make the operations verbs actually track manufacturing coordinators (match however they are registered), or make them error loudly pointing at container verbs — a verb that silently reports 'nothing running' while the thing runs is an observability lie. RETIRES L53's second half on landing.
close_reason: merged 42dd937, DEPLOYED (build-cli), LIVE-VERIFIED: 'operations status' now shows parallel_manufacturing coordinator + mfg-task worker under Manufacturing->Coordinators/Workers (was 'Other Containers'). ROOT CAUSE: single case-mismatch — daemon persists UPPERCASE domain types (MANUFACTURING_COORDINATOR), CLI matched lowercase substrings, so strings.Contains never hit; also silently broke GAS + 'operations stop --manufacturing'. FIX: classify against container.ContainerType* constants via strings.EqualFold, shared predicates for status+stop (can't disagree again), PARALLEL_MANUFACTURING matched. Construction surfaces here too (runs through the mfg coordinator, no own container). 4 tests, 5 subcases, all RED-proven. Retires lessons.md L53 second-half ('operations --manufacturing don't track, use container stop') + strategy.md d-65 stop-path guard — flag sp-4ek3.

title: Detector tuning: exempt in-transit ships from heartbeat_lost; add crash-LOOP signal distinct from single crashes
description: TIER-3 (watchkeeper detectors), Admiral PRE-APPROVED 2026-07-07. Two detector refinements, same file as sp-1hak (coordinate — land after/with it): (a) L29: heartbeat_lost fires on slow solar scouts whose transit leg exceeds the window — exempt ships whose position is ADVANCING (or IN_TRANSIT) from stale-heartbeat, or use per-role windows; a frozen position is the real signal. (b) L40: a single crash is self-healing (auto-restart+resume) — the actionable signal is a crash-LOOP (restart count climbing with no progress). sp-okwk made container.crashed count true deaths; ADD a distinct crash-loop detection (e.g. container.crashloop interrupt when N true deaths of the same container within window W, or restart_count climbing without progress) so the captain is interrupted for loops, not singles. TEST: transit-legged scout emits nothing; 3 rapid true deaths of one container emit exactly one crashloop interrupt. RETIRES L29 and L40's escalation clause on landing.
close_reason: merged e6682b7, DEPLOYED: watchkeeper rebuilt pid 92240. Two tunings (both TDD RED-proven, sp-1hak cooldown + sp-t0f6 rename respected): (a) stale-heartbeat EXEMPTS in-transit ships — a frozen position is the real death signal, a transit-legged scout no longer false-fires (reuses detectIdleShips' quoted-symbol join); (b) crash-LOOP vs single-crash — new container.crashloop type, detectCrashLoops emits ONE interrupt when >=3 true deaths (per sp-okwk's now-truthful count) of one container in 30m, deduped via HasSince; container.crashed DEMOTED from the DEFAULT interrupt set (single crash defers, self-heals). CRITICAL LIVE-BEHAVIOR NOTE: the captain's DECLARED interrupt override still lists container.crashed and REPLACES the default set (sk68 IsInterrupt) — so its live behavior is UNCHANGED by the default-set demotion; to ADOPT crash-loop-only waking it must re-declare with container.crashloop. Mailing captain. Full gates green. Retires lessons L29 (heartbeat/frozen-position) + L40 escalation clause — flagged for sp-4ek3. Config-tunable window/threshold filed sp-d3xi P3.

title: Schema-drift CI gate: register-all-models check so the 42703 missing-migration class can't recur
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L54's own named durable fix, never built: startup AutoMigrate is a hand-maintained model list that omits whole subsystems — the missing-migration 42703 class recurred TWICE (s73 ships.reserved, s77 manufacturing sequence_number; only per-model additive patches landed, e.g. 5095c78). BUILD: register ALL persistence models in one place (or generate the AutoMigrate list) + a CI check that fails when a GORM model exists without a migration/AutoMigrate registration (schema-drift gate). A schema-drift defect is invisible to a 'launches clean' check — the CI gate is the only durable guard. RETIRES the L54 hazard (curation bead compresses it to a recurrence-watch line).
close_reason: merged 55025f4. Generalized 23514 CHECK-constraint enum drift gate: 5 constraints (task_type/task_status/pipeline_status/operation_type/storage_status) each domain-enum <= migration-defined set, parser resolves highest-numbered migration (033 wins DROP+re-ADD). DURABILITY: reverse-completeness guard — any NEW enum CHECK constraint added later without a domain mapping FAILS the gate (stops recurrence, which was the point). TDD teeth self-test proves the detector fires on seeded drift. Folded in + deleted the sp-8ind single-constraint guard (strictly stronger: exact-set + obsolete-token rejection vs the old subset-only). 512 tests green, gate green. Test-only, no daemon deploy. Findings preserved: TaskStatusCancelled correctly excluded (never persisted), trade_type/pipeline_type have no DB CHECK at all.

title: Naming collision: supervisor daemon is named 'captain' (same as the agent it wakes) + 'watchkeeper' as a third alias
description: PURE RENAME (no behavior change) to end a naming collision that has real operator cost. The supervisor daemon built from gobot/cmd/captain -> gobot/bin/captain (Go package captainsup) is NOT the captain agent — it is the background process that watches the fleet DB every ~30s and wakes/pokes the captain AI session. So 'captain' names TWO distinct things: (1) the supervisor daemon (gobot/bin/captain, pid observed 20880), (2) the operator AGENT (a gc/Claude session it wakes). Worse, the crew templates AND the surveyor charter call this same daemon 'the watchkeeper' — a THIRD name for the same thing. Three names, two things. EVIDENCE OF COST: this collision made the Admiral lose the thread during a live P1 auto-restart discussion 2026-07-07 ('what the fuck is the watchkeeper'); it also makes logs, greps, and onboarding ambiguous (captain-supervisor.log lines are prefixed 'captain:' for the DAEMON, not the agent). FIX: pick ONE unambiguous name for the supervisor daemon and use it everywhere, reserving 'captain' exclusively for the agent. Recommend 'watchkeeper' (already the conceptual name in the templates) or 'supervisor'. Rename the binary (gobot/bin/*), cmd dir (gobot/cmd/captain), Go package (captainsup), log prefixes, config comments, AND the crew templates + surveyor charter that say 'watchkeeper' — so code and prose converge on one term. CAVEAT: the running daemon logs to captain-supervisor.log with 'captain:' prefixes; the rename must update any dashboards/monitors/log-greps that key on those strings (e.g. captain-dashboard.py, supervisor log tails). TIER-3: touches the watchkeeper AND templates -> human sign-off on scope before it moves. Admiral-requested 2026-07-07.
notes:
Admiral approved 2026-07-07 (Tier-3 sign-off). Rename cleared to work; 'human' label removed. Touches the watchkeeper + templates — worktree + captain-gate discipline; pure rename, no behavior change.

Deploy: plist ProgramArguments flipped (label + BD_REAL + log path kept), stale bin/captain removed. Kept by design: agent-meaning 'captain' everywhere, CLI captain verbs, captain-gate, captain: config key, com.spacetraders.captain label, applied-migration comment (immutable), historical design docs. Gate hiccups en route: foreign template WIP (stash dance, preserved) + a UU .beads/issues.jsonl resolved to HEAD (generated file). 'captain' now names exactly one thing: the agent.

title: Watchkeeper/gate hardening batch: backoff, double-log, liveness, silent crash-drop, latent edges
description: From a full read-only code review of the captain engine (surveyor-triaged; shipwright verifies each before fixing). Six distinct TIER-3 (watchkeeper/gate) items: (1) Delivery backoff can't back off a dead channel while any interrupt is queued — wake.go:187-192 caps retry at 2xpoll when hasUnmailedInterrupt, so a total gc/bd outage with a standing interrupt retries every ~60s forever; the exponential/15-min ceiling never engages. Fix: full backoff even for interrupts when preflight/SessionAlive shows the channel fully down. (2) Every failed wake logs twice — supervisor.go:224-226 calls noteDeliveryFailure ('WAKE DELIVERY FAILING') AND returns the same err ('tick error'), diluting the grep signal. Fix: return nil once noted. (3) SessionAlive over-eager — gc.go:84 treats any non active/running state as dead -> double-spawn risk on transient startup. (4) nil captainRecorder silently drops crashes — captain_recorder.go:28 returns early when rec==nil with no startup warning; a mis-wired recorder makes container.crashed (and all synthetic events) vanish invisibly (same class as sp-vvnw). (5) Spurious credits.threshold wake on credit-source switch — supervisor.go:159-160 prevCredits baseline; refreshCredits (agentcredits.go:64) can flip ledger<->live between polls, firing a false interrupt wake on the first live read after fallback/restart. (6) Gate hardcodes literal 'main' — worktree.go:110-113 (merge-base --is-ancestor main); any repo whose default branch isn't 'main' has every merge refused as stale (latent). Citations from code review, surveyor spot-checked. TIER-3 — human sign-off.

title: Surveyor cadence stamped before mail delivery: one transient error skips a whole review cycle
description: nudgeSurveyorOnCadence (gobot/internal/captain/surveyor_nudge.go:32-37) sets s.lastSurveyorNudge = now (l.33) and persists it BEFORE calling SendMail (l.34); on mail error it just returns (l.35-36) with no log. A single transient mail failure advances the cadence clock as if the survey ran — silently skipping a full meta_review_days cycle (surveyor simply isn't woken until the next interval). Contrast the wake path, which stamps only after success. Surveyor-verified against source 2026-07-07 (my own wake path). FIX: stamp lastSurveyorNudge only after SendMail succeeds; log the failure. TIER-3 (watchkeeper) — human sign-off.

title: Task executor swallows errors on ALL task types - defect-#6 fix covered only the supply call (2 blind failures today)
description: Evidence: (1) defect-#6 era: 4 construction supply failures with zero error detail at any log level - fixed for that call site only (verbatim error logging landed there). (2) 05:35:38 today: COLLECT_SELL fail on T7 (phantom-cargo resume branch, L47 family) - again just 'ERROR: Manufacturing task failed', no underlying error; diagnosis required manual ship refresh + inference. Ask: wrap the task-runner's error path generically - log task type, step, ship, and the underlying error verbatim for EVERY task failure. One wrap at the executor dispatch level covers all current and future task types. Acceptance: any manufacturing/construction task failure logs the root error string.
close_reason: Merged bc3a952: manufacturing task-failure now logs the verbatim cause+type+ship in the MESSAGE (was bare 'Manufacturing task failed' with cause in the dropped metadata field). One wrap at the dispatch failure path surfaces all task types (COLLECT_SELL, ACQUIRE_DELIVER, etc.); executor audit confirms none swallow. Generalizes fi7q. Full gate green. Daemon-side — batches into next make restart-daemon.

title: coordinator logs failed workers as completed at INFO; ContractsCompleted counts failures
description: Follow-up from sp-okwk (fix-okwk investigation, 2026-07-07). run_fleet_coordinator.go: all four worker-completion receive sites (:153, :174, :262, :338) log at INFO and never inspect WorkerCompletedEvent.Success/.Error (fields exist, navigation/events.go:10-11) — a crashed worker is logged as a completion; the respawn line (:403 'Starting worker container for %s') doesn't distinguish crash-respawn from new-task. ALSO: those sites do result.ContractsCompleted++ even for failed workers (e.g. :340) — the completed-contracts metric over-counts. Fix: log event.Error at ERROR when !event.Success; label respawn reason; increment ContractsCompleted only on Success. Worker-side crash signature is already fixed by sp-okwk (recordCrash).

title: player info echoes full API token to stdout
description: Observed live 2026-07-07 post sp-900v: 'spacetraders player info' prints the full JWT token to stdout. Pre-existing display behavior (sp-900v only fixed resolution/injection). With sp-900v the captain now runs this verb routinely for treasury reads -> tokens accumulate in session logs/transcripts. Fix: mask the token (prefix+...) or drop it behind a --show-token flag. Also cosmetic: 'Player ID: {2}' prints the PlayerID struct with braces (%v on shared.PlayerID) — fix formatting in the same pass. Both are display-only, no logic change.

title: 3 specialist templates falsely promise 'watchkeeper respawns you' on rollover
description: shipwright/prompt.template.md:72-73, fleet-architect/prompt.template.md:76, and trade-analyst/prompt.template.md:76 each instruct the agent to 'gc handoff' on rollover and promise 'the watchkeeper respawns you.' The watchkeeper does NOT respawn specialists. It respawns ONLY the captain (ensureCaptainAlive -> SpawnSession(s.cfg.CaptainAgent), gobot/internal/captain/respawn.go:15,20) and spawns the surveyor on cadence (surveyor_nudge.go:26-31). For specialists it only reopens orphaned pipeline beads (respawn.go:28 requeueOrphanedPipelineBeads) - it never restarts a session. So a specialist that hands off mid-work is never reawoken; the work stalls silently until a human or a fresh consult re-invokes it. FIX: correct the three TEMPLATES to state real behavior (handoff bead persists; session is re-invoked manually / on next consult). TIER-3 rail (templates) - human sign-off required.

title: Restart orphans in-flight contract worker containers - generalize restart-state reconciliation (session-89 class)
description: From the Admiral's 2026-07-06 handoff (st-wisp-a1q, open thread 1; source: captain session 89): a deploy/restart kills in-flight contract worker containers. Restart recovery skips them because their state is in-memory only, leaving the coordinator looping 'no ships available'. Same disease class as the fix-pipeline orphan bug already fixed via RecoverOrphanedFixes() (supervisor resets stranded in_progress fix reports on startup - built pre-shutdown, goes live on next captain start). Generalize 'restart reconciles orphaned state' across BOTH the fixer and the container runner. Filed by harbormaster; candidate for captain grooming / shipwright after era-2 bring-up (st-wm7).
notes:
Groomed at era-2 bring-up (2026-07-06): disease class fired again in inverse form - restart recovery resurrected DEAD-era player-1 coordinators (see sp-njpu). Era-1 workers self-terminated cleanly; the coordinators had to be stopped by hand. Restart-state reconciliation needs to be era/player-liveness aware in BOTH directions: don't kill live workers, don't revive dead eras.
Third field occurrence (2026-07-06 23:35 restart, c2bc deploy): T3's in-flight contract worker died in the restart and did NOT resume - T3 came back idle/unassigned (0 cargo, no container). Coordinator self-healed by claiming T6 (the mfg-earmarked hull) - fleet role-shuffle as a side effect. Observation for the fix design: with identical hulls the allocation policy degrades gracefully to STREAM COUNTS (1 contract + 2 mfg), and coordinators rebalance by discovery - so the orphan class costs minutes of one stream running under-count, not stranded cargo. Still needs the reconciliation fix for the mid-contract-with-cargo case (era-1 saw stranded cargo + 'no ships available' loops).

title: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tas
description: friction: (s85) `construction start` is a SILENT FOOTGUN: it creates the pipeline row (pipeline_type=CONSTRUCTION) + tasks and returns "Status: PLANNING", but spawns NO coordinator container and returns NO container id — the pipeline sits INERT at 0.0% forever with nothing executing it. The executor is the MANUFACTURING coordinator, which adopts pipelines ONLY at startup recovery (`FindByStatus(PLANNING,EXECUTING)`, `run_parallel_manufacturing_coordinator.go` recoverState at ~line 200) and NEVER re-polls the table in its main loop. So a construction pipeline created AFTER the coordinator booted is never seen. The undocumented required sequence is: `construction start` FIRST, THEN bounce `operations --manufacturing` so recovery adopts it. Candidate fix: either (a) `construction start` should signal the running coordinator to re-scan (or spawn its own coordinator), or (b) the coordinator loop should periodically re-poll for new PLANNING pipelines, or at minimum (c) `construction start` should WARN "no coordinator running — start operations --manufacturing to execute this pipeline". Nothing about the success output hints the pipeline is inert.

title: friction: (s85) No way to PRIORITIZE mission/gate (CONSTRUCTION) tasks over income (manufacturing) tasks — the manufactu
description: friction: (s85) No way to PRIORITIZE mission/gate (CONSTRUCTION) tasks over income (manufacturing) tasks — the manufacturing coordinator runs a UNIFIED task queue across both pipeline types with a shared `--max-workers` pool and no per-pipeline worker pinning or priority. A large manufacturing backlog (10-14 queued COLLECT_SELL tasks) can starve the gate-build tasks of workers even though the gate is the higher mission priority. Candidate fix: a per-pipeline-type priority/weight, or a `construction start --dedicate <ship>` that pins a hauler, so mission-critical construction isn't forced to compete on equal footing with income tasks.

A `ledger list --category TRADING_REVENUE --by-ship` or a per-container P&L rollup (revenue − costs − fuel, tagged by the worker container that generated it) would make stream-level $/h a one-command read instead of a cross-timezone reconciliation.

Candidate fix: a `construction start --dry-run` that reports estimated material cost + task count, and/or a `--budget` cap, so the biggest single mission spend isn't launched blind.

title: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing
description: friction: (s83) `operations status` and `operations stop --manufacturing` do NOT track a NORMALLY-launched manufacturing coordinator (no restart involved) — generalizes the s75 restart-storm item. Right after `operations start --manufacturing` succeeded and the coordinator + worker were RUNNING, `operations status` printed "No active manufacturing operations" and listed the coordinator under "Other Containers", and `operations stop --manufacturing` returned "No matching operations to stop". So the d-65 GUARD (stop the engine if it grabs the contract hauler) is non-functional as written — the only working stop is `container stop <coordinator-id>`. Candidate fix: `operations status`/`operations stop` should classify containers by type from the container DB (name prefix `parallel_manufacturing-` / recorded operation kind), not from in-memory session tracking, so a manufacturing op is stoppable/observable via its own verb whether or not a restart intervened.
