title: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH —
description: friction: (s75) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE (build+tests) IS BLIND TO A COORDINATOR THAT CRASHES THE WHOLE DAEMON ON LAUNCH — same class as the s73 missing-migration blind spot. `operations start --manufacturing` panics the daemon in a restart loop because the parallel-manufacturing handler's `eventSubscriber`/`eventPublisher` are never wired in main.go (they're nil), and the command runs on a naked goroutine with no recover() (L53, report d-83). Tests pass; a live launch takes down the process. CANDIDATE FIXES: (a) a gate smoke-test that actually launches EACH coordinator type against a throwaway daemon and asserts it survives ≥1 iteration past state-recovery; (b) a top-level `recover()` in ContainerRunner.execute() so no single coordinator panic can ever kill the whole daemon (containment — turns a process-death into a container FAILED).

title: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged
description: friction: (s73) THE FIX-PIPELINE GATE CANNOT CATCH A MISSING-MIGRATION REGRESSION. The reservation feature (d-79) merged with green tests yet took the WHOLE contract earner down in production: it added GORM struct tags (Reserved/ReservationReason on ShipModel) but no SQL migration, and the test DB AutoMigrates tags while production requires hand-applied migrations/*.sql that were never authored (SQLSTATE 42703 on every ship Save; L52). The build+test gate is structurally blind to this class. CANDIDATE FIX (high value — this outage cost hours of zero income): add a schema-drift check to the gate that compares GORM model tags against the applied `migrations/` DDL (or runs the production migration path in CI) and FAILS a schema-touching feature that lacks its migration. Second-order gap: the Captain has no way to apply a DB migration (CLI-only actuator, no psql) — a migration fix requires an out-of-band operator step even after code merges, so schema fixes have a longer restore-latency than pure-code fixes; a `spacetraders admin migrate` verb (or auto-run migrations on daemon boot) would let the daemon self-repair schema drift on restart.

title: shipyard purchase --waypoint false-fails on empty cache instead of refreshing
description: Admiral-approved 2026-07-07. L49: 'shipyard purchase' with a pinned --waypoint FALSE-FAILS against an empty shipyard cache (reads stale/empty cache and reports failure instead of refreshing); the workaround folklore is 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint so auto-discover visits and reads fresh'. FIX: on empty/stale cache for an explicitly pinned waypoint, refresh (visit/read) before failing — an explicit waypoint should be MORE reliable than auto-discovery, not less. RETIRES L49's re-buy clause on landing.
close_reason: merged 05df3e3. Real defect (agent correctly ignored my brief's case/trait speculation, diagnosed from bead): 'shipyard purchase --waypoint' false-failed on a FRESH shipyard. SpaceTraders /shipyard omits the priced 'ships' array until one of your ships is present at the waypoint (returns only 'shipTypes'); the batch handler read listings UP FRONT for budget math BEFORE the purchase loop navigates there -> FindListingByType miss -> hard abort 'ship type not available'. FIX: getShipPriceFromShipyard returns listingReady bool, discriminated via always-present shipTypes — HasShipType+empty-priced = empty cache -> DEFER (purchasableCount=Quantity, keep pinned waypoint so the loop visits+reads fresh, bounded by per-purchase credit guard); type absent from shipTypes = genuine error, fail fast. Symmetric with auto-discover, which dodged this by skipping the pre-read (the 're-buy WITHOUT --waypoint' folklore, L49). 3 TDD tests incl. over-defer guard, RED-proven, gates green. value: Captain wake model (sp-sk68, live 2026-07-07): the captain OWNS its cadence — declare next-wake time / credit thresholds / interrupt-types at the end of each wake via 'spacetraders captain wake set ...' Contracts doctrine (consolidates L6/L8/L21/L48, and L45). Contracts pay TWICE (accept + deliver) and are guaranteed — prefer them over speculative mining/trading in bootstrap (L6). Source contract goods from EXPORT markets (cheapest); avoid IMPORT buys; mine only when no market option (L8). batch-contract / purchase-planning FAILS FAST without cached market data — sequence: scout-all → data gathered → THEN batch-contract (L21). ECONOMICS (L48/L45): contract cycle time is TRAVEL-dominated (~67%) and cadence is ENDOGENOUS — decompose ledger timestamps before calling $/h "supply-gated." The coordinator runs ONE contract at a time, but a 2nd hauler compresses mean buy-leg distance = more cycles/hour even one-at-a-time (extra haulers cut cycle time, not just positioning). It logs per-contract provider DISTANCE + cargo (the closest thing to a `contract list` verb). Compute NET by hand-pairing ledger rows; gross payout overstates by ~30%. Speed-blind "select closest" is a leak ONLY with 2+ eligible light haulers.
L23 [d-6] — The event feed emits MULTIPLE rows per failure (one retry burst); group by container_id+ts before treating as a new incident, fix root cause once.
L36 [d-16] — A multi-session HOLD needs a falsifiable off-ramp: use report status (L35) + an explicit exit condition. A bounded HOLD beats an open-ended one.

L46 [d-32,d-34,d-65] — The parallel trade/manufacturing lever is NOT capital-gated. Actuator (ship sell) LIFTED s28; ship-buy ask WITHDRAWN (the `operations --manufacturing` engine IS the acquisition path). Real gates: FLEET CAPACITY (a dedicated hauler, assignment-excluded — no flag needed) + sell-side volume (6–20 SCARCE, L13) + validation (L16).

The supervisor daemon is the WATCHKEEPER (bin/watchkeeper, cmd/watchkeeper, package watchkeeper in internal/captain dir, 'watchkeeper:' log prefixes, banner 'Watchkeeper starting') since sp-t0f6 (2026-07-07). 'captain' = ONLY the agent. Deploy: make build-watchkeeper (build-captain aliases to it); launchd label REMAINS com.spacetraders.captain; log file remains captain-supervisor.log. The plist (~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.spacetraders.captain.plist) runs bin/watchkeeper and MUST carry BD_REAL=/Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/bin/bd: the bd-router shim in ~/.local/bin shadows the real bd and the plist PATH lacks ~/IdeaProjects/bin, so without BD_REAL every captain wake fails silently (gc mail -> bd list -> exit 127). Fleet purchase sequencing: the rule-3 >25%-treasury consult threshold is denominator-relative, so identical ships get MORE expensive against the guardrail as you spend. Ex (era torwind, X1-KA42): a ~257k light hauler was 24.2% at 1.04M treasury (slipped under) but 34.9% at 737k after the 1st buy (binds rule 3). Sequence multi-ship buys worst-ratio-first, or expect later ones to trip the consult gate. Also: a contract pool with one-contract-at-a-time semantics caps a 2nd contract-hauler's value at POSITIONING (closest-ship shorter buy legs), not parallel throughput — measure the lift before scaling the contract side further. And sp-c2bc-class per-ship planner bugs multiply respawn/replan overhead linearly with fleet size, so fix them before growing the fleet.

Spawned fix-agents share this session's $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp — leftover scratch files (e.g. a prior task's commitmsg.txt) can be read by a later agent and cause wrong commit messages/inputs. fix-r3cl hit this (read a stale hs2j commitmsg), caught+amended it. Construction & manufacturing doctrine (consolidates L53/L57/L59 + recurrence-watch for fixed L54/L55). The construction/manufacturing pipeline was a CHAIN of never-exercised-subsystem defects (s82–s91), ALL now fixed and deployed: schema/valid_task_type (sp-8ind; missing-migration 42703 fix, commit 5095c78/L54), pipeline adoption without a manual coordinator bounce (sp-hkfb), DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION dispatch (sp-mvcr), task replenishment (sp-b1np), planner all-or-nothing across materials (sp-r900), one-leg-fail terminalization (sp-hs2j), and operations status/stop being blind to manufacturing (sp-dpsq). DURABLE ARCHITECTURE (still true): construction pipelines are `ManufacturingPipelineModel` rows discriminated by `pipeline_type='CONSTRUCTION'` — the SAME manufacturing_pipelines table, SAME persistence path, and the SAME task queue + single `--max-workers` pool as manufacturing (no per-pipeline pinning), so a manufacturing backlog can STARVE gate/construction tasks. The MANUFACTURING coordinator is the executor for BOTH. Per-task queue priority: DELIVER_TO_CONSTRUCTION 75 > COLLECT_SELL 50 > ACQUIRE_DELIVER 10. Observe/halt manufacturing via `container logs` / `container stop <parallel_manufacturing-… id>` (operations status/stop now track it too, sp-dpsq); a heavy coordinator launch that restart-LOOPS the DAEMON shows as the CONTAINER's RestartCount climbing while the daemon itself dies. VERIFY a pipeline/gate by MATERIAL progress climbing off 0.0% (first observable OUTPUT), NOT by pipeline status "EXECUTING" — that proves only PERSISTENCE+ADOPTION, not execution (see verification-doctrine). RECURRENCE-WATCH: missing-migration 42703 recurs whenever a new subsystem's models are omitted from the hand-maintained startup AutoMigrate list — fix = add the model (additive, self-heals on restart, no psql); durable fix = a CI schema-drift check / register ALL models; a schema-drift defect is invisible to a "launches clean" check, so verify a stream by an actual PERSIST/claim. Before betting a mission thread on any never-before-run subsystem, read the composition root for activation + executor registration, or demand first observable OUTPUT within one window.

Captain conduct doctrine (pointer). The FIVE standing conduct corrections — (1) constraint audit (every self-imposed limit carries a NAMED expiry, re-checked each wake), (2) measurement windows = opportunity-cost math not round "day marks", (3) grade on the evidence TREND not the review_after date, (4) ownership audit before any manual fleet action (allocation policy binds the captain too), (5) sensing = the wake model is the only standing sensor (never poll between wakes) — are taught in FULL in the captain prompt template's "Conduct doctrine" section (Admiral-corrected 2026-07-07) and applied every wake. This memory is only a pointer to prevent re-filing; the template is the source of truth. Two phrasings the template compresses, kept verbatim: "filed-as-decided is where evidence goes to die" (constraint-audit / deferral bias); long operations run as fire-and-forget background chains (sensing).
L27 [d-7,d-8] — container-RUNNING ≠ data-populated; gate a dependent workflow on the feeder's `workflow.finished`/COMPLETED, not merely RUNNING.

Verification doctrine (spine = L62; consolidates L20/L39/L42/L52/L60/L62). Merged is a CLAIM, not a result: declare a capability unblocked ONLY on its first observable OUTPUT — material/credits/rows — never on a merged fix. Exercise IMMEDIATELY after every merge, and re-verify capability state EVERY session (it flips) by testing permitted commands LIVE — don't trust stale "degraded" notes or a report's stale numbers (L20). Confirm by EXERCISING observed behavior, not status alone (L39). Never-exercised subsystems fail in CHAINS (s83-s91 construction: missing migration → no activation/executor → poisoned 0-task pipeline → over-strict market filter): plan for N>1 blockers and keep each probe cheap. Three ways "merged" lies: (1) a merged DAEMON fix takes effect only after the daemon RESTARTS (L39); (2) a client-side CLI panic can persist from a STALE BINARY after its fix merges — re-verify by exercising, reopen merged→new on recurrence (L42; `ship sell` confirmed crash-safe s28); (3) a merged feature with GREEN tests can still break production on SCHEMA — test DBs auto-migrate from GORM tags, production relies on hand-written migrations, so on a schema-touching `feat` re-check the earner next session and confirm the fix reached the LIVE DB (a 42703 self-heals once the column exists — leave the coordinator running, idempotent failed Saves) (L52). And a merged code fix does NOT repair the STATE the buggy binary already left behind: expect poisoned persisted rows as a SECOND, independent blocker (s87: an EXECUTING CONSTRUCTION pipeline whose tasks restart-recovery had reaped), and idempotent "resume" verbs turn that state into a permanent trap (StartOrResume returns any PLANNING/EXECUTING pipeline as-is — --depth ignored, no re-plan, nothing terminalizes a 0-task pipeline). So verify the DATA precondition (do the tasks/rows exist?) in the same breath as the code path, and put "tasks exist" BEFORE "output > 0" in the expectation chain (L60). Corollary: when a start verb fails on a market/data precondition, read the exact filter threshold in CODE before waiting for the world to change — the tolerant variant may already exist one function away (FindExportMarketBySupplyPriority vs FindExportMarketWithGoodSupply) (L62).

L40 [d-20,d-21,d-59] — A single 4203 fuel-crash (and transient API-error/404 bursts) is SELF-HEALING: the container auto-restarts, refuels, resumes; the coordinator re-spawns a fresh worker after a burst window. Don't intervene on the first crash — escalate only on a crash-LOOP (restart count climbing, no progress) or a ship idle >60min WITH cargo. Judge a route by NET ledger P&L, not fuel-line alarm.

HARD RULE (Admiral, 2026-07-07, emphatic): NEVER touch gc code. The gc gateway CLI (source: /Users/andres.dandrea/IdeaProjects/gascity-src, module github.com/gastownhall/gascity) is OFF-LIMITS to the shipwright — do not read-to-modify, branch, build, patch, or install it, even for an Admiral-visible bug, even if a bead asks. gc bugs get surfaced to the Admiral and left for the gc owner. Market & trading doctrine (consolidates L1/L2/L3/L4/L5/L9/L13/L15/L16/L18 + hauler-size-to-market-absorption + spacetraders-market-columns). ⚠ COLUMN ORIENTATION — misread this and you INVERT every margin (~2x overstatement): SpaceTraders market columns are from the MARKET's perspective (verified by realized trades, era-2, 3 circuits) — BUY PRICE = the market's bid = what YOU RECEIVE per unit selling TO it; SELL PRICE = the market's ask = what YOU PAY per unit buying FROM it. So profit/unit = destination BUY price − source SELL price. Core arbitrage: buy at EXPORTERS, sell at IMPORTERS (L9) — the most reliable edge. PRICE INTEL: there are NO market prices without a ship physically AT the waypoint — scouting is the only source (L2). Keep 1 (cheap) probe per 2–3 markets for freshness before committing haulers (L1); prefer SOLAR probes as scouts (zero fuel = infinite runtime, they pay for themselves fast) (L4); deploy ALL scouts to cover ALL markets — never leave one idle or scout only a subset (L3). ROUTES decay: routes aren't static, competition equilibrates prices — keep MULTIPLE routes and exit a lane once margin drops below threshold (L15); declining $/h at steady yields = saturation from selling the same export, so diversify or cut volume (L13); fuel itself is a volatile exchange good — high-demand waypoints spike and can kill a route's margin (L18). Always compute ROUND-TRIP fuel (out + return + 10% margin); a stranded ship earns zero (L5). SCALING: validate profitability BEFORE buying ships — premature scaling depletes credits (L16). Size haulers to MARKET ABSORPTION, not the biggest hold available: a fat per-unit spread means nothing if trade VOLUME is thin (torwind X1-KA42 mfg import SHIP_PLATING/PARTS at A2 showed ~179% gross spread — buy ~2504 at D40 → sell ~6982 at A2 — but VOLUME 6 / WEAK activity, so the market absorbs ~6 units/tick before price craters). An 80-cargo light hauler (257k) is oversized for a single volume-6 destination; an 88k light shuttle matches deliverable throughput for ~169k less UNLESS one run batches across multiple import markets. Read destination VOLUME + activity before buying a big hauler — cargo capacity past the market's absorption rate is dead weight; check spread AND volume TOGETHER.

STANDING DIRECTIVE (2026-07-06, Admiral, emphatic, while going AFK): NEVER depend on the Admiral or block on their input. This is an autonomous fleet/city system that MUST run fully unattended. Do NOT queue decisions, design rulings, gate approvals, memory gates, reload/deploy choices, or 'which option?' questions to the Admiral and wait — that is a design failure. Make the best call yourself from strategy + judgment, ACT, and surface results for ASYNC review only (bd notes/mail), never as a blocking question. Route all agent escalations to harbormaster, never to human. Only involve the human for genuine catastrophe or truly irreversible external action, and even then act first to keep the fleet safe. Supersedes any prior pattern of asking the Admiral to sign off.

Financial guards must be counter-cyclical: a min-balance that freezes income-GENERATING working capital amplifies the drawdown it guards against (engine stops earning exactly when cash is tightest). Set engine spend-floors BELOW the captain's assessment tripwire so the human/captain decision fires before the engine hard-stops, and size floors against working-capital cycle time (spend returns within the hour), not against fear.

L25 [d-3,d-4,d-7] — Launch heavy workflows ONE AT A TIME (absorbs L22): CONCURRENT launches hung the socket (launching-while-another-RUNS is fine). Launch → confirm health + RUNNING → next; never two at once.

L35 [d-14,d-74] — Bug-report status is the pipeline-progress observable: new → in_progress → gate_failed (re-queuable) → awaiting_human → merged. A status is a snapshot not a ratchet; a re-queued/long-blocked report DOES eventually land (feature blocked s53→s67 then shipped). Pipeline is NOT priority-aware.

Manual trade-circuit chains MUST verify before trading: (1) after any navigate step, confirm the ship's actual location equals the intended market before buying/selling - a failed nav that the chain treats as complete buys at the WRONG market at destination prices; (2) re-read the live ask before committing a buy and abort the circuit if it exceeds the cached basis by >30% - cached spreads go stale in minutes on active lanes; (3) completion-greps must distinguish success from failure tokens, never 'finished|failed|ERROR' as one bucket that always proceeds.

bd quirk: 'bd init --prefix X' sets only the dolt database name; the ISSUE prefix is db config key issue_prefix (underscore), which init leaves at its default - set it explicitly with 'bd config set issue_prefix X' before creating any bead, and verify with a probe create
L29 [d-8] — heartbeat_lost on a slow solar scout is usually a transit leg exceeding the window, not a zombie; if position advanced + restart_count 0, leave it. Stop only if position is frozen.

L43 [d-23,d-25] — batch-contract runs ONE contract regardless of --iterations N. For continuous operation use `contract start` (runs until stopped); a COMMAND ship qualifies as fallback when no hauler exists.

L49 [d-37,d-78] — To PRICE a shipyard, physically dock a ship there (scout tours populate market goods, not ship listings). The command ship's post-fulfillment idle gap is a SAFE borrow-window for side-errands. LIGHT_HAULER = 2× cargo, ~0.4× speed vs command — measure $/h, don't assume. Re-buy WITHOUT `--waypoint` (auto-discover visits+reads fresh); a pinned `--waypoint` false-fails on an empty shipyard cache.

Ledger & measurement doctrine (consolidates L28/L31/L41/L44/L56). TREASURY: anchor real treasury to the last CONTRACT_* ledger row + the sum of subsequent AMOUNTS; the fleet-report Credits field lags a fulfillment (L40) (raw telemetry was garbage pre-s6, now mostly fixed — still sanity-check). GROUND TRUTH cuts BOTH ways: `workflow.finished success:true` is NOT proof of completion, but an empty/stale ledger is NOT proof of a stall either — the ledger QUERY (Postgres) can lag container events by HOURS, so the container log's explicit `Contract fulfillment transaction recorded in ledger` line is FRESHER than the ledger read. Trust the container log for whether a fulfillment happened; treat a "3h gap" in `ledger list` as lag until the log disproves it. A socket hang costs OBSERVABILITY, not money: the coordinator still commits to DB/server while the socket is wedged (treasury climbed through a hang) — don't stop it; it's a money problem ONLY if no new CONTRACT_* rows appear. ISOLATING $/h: `ledger list --type SELL_CARGO` cleanly isolates manufacturing/trading revenue — contracts emit CONTRACT_FULFILLED and NEVER SELL_CARGO, so gross trading revenue = sum(SELL_CARGO) with zero hand-pairing (only the BUY side stays muddied — needs hand-pairing, gross overstates ~30%). Two gotchas: (a) the ledger displays in UTC-3 while container logs are UTC — align a sell to its task by the exact 3h offset (sell 21:53:20 local == task-complete 00:53:20 UTC); (b) a factory's FIRST collection dumps ACCUMULATED inventory, so the first COLLECT_SELL rate OVERSTATES steady state (several× the steady rate — never annualize one draw). Likewise contract payouts are LUMPY (one netted +155k, ~24× typical): derive $/h from SEVERAL contracts, not one draw, and size the 50% spend guardrail against the biggest plausible cargo buy (~73k+), not the average.

spacetraders CLI ops (current, 2026-07-07; supersedes the old gobot-cwd rule): the CLI is on PATH with any-cwd config resolution (sp-b07s/ccd84a5) - run 'spacetraders' from anywhere; the old 'must run from gobot/' trap (SASL/connection-refused outside gobot/) is DEAD. Default player persists via 'config set-player --player-id N' (~/.spacetraders/config.json) but some verbs still demand explicit --player-id (ledger list, contract list). Treasury: 'player info' works (sp-900v) but echoes the raw player token in output until sp-byza lands - do NOT paste its output; use the ledger list balance column as the safe treasury read. Shipyard listings show prices only when one of YOUR ships is docked at that yard (an API visibility rule, not an empty yard).

L58 [d-90,s86] — "Not sold in-system" is a TIME-STAMPED observation, not standing truth: factory markets wake their export side once anyone (including YOUR OWN pipelines) feeds their imports. Goods that a spot-check of a few waypoints had recorded as "unavailable" reappeared with live export supply elsewhere in the system two sessions later — flipping the gate plan from a multi-hour fabrication campaign to a far cheaper direct buy. Re-sweep `market get` across the system before locking any plan premise (depth selection, "must fabricate", sourcing) to a stale availability claim; the sweep costs ~30 reads, the wrong premise costs sessions.

Socket & backends doctrine (consolidates L19/L30). TWO backends: the socket (health/ship/container/workflow) is the DAEMON; market/ledger/player are POSTGRES. SQLSTATE errors on the Postgres side while the socket still answers = a DB outage, NOT daemon death — keep operating on socket data. A socket hang (context deadline exceeded) can be spontaneous and may not self-recover in-session. Scope it with ONE socket probe + ONE ledger probe (if the ledger answers, the daemon isn't dead); don't hand-probe repeatedly (token burn) — record, defer, it recovers between sessions. A multi-session blackout can be an out-of-band ops event, not a code bug.

Cache-desync doctrine (recurrence-watch; consolidates L32/L37/L47/L50 + L61 refresh-pool clause). `ship info` cargo/position/role can be a PHANTOM (stale cache) diverging from the SERVER, which is ground truth (delivery/sell API). It RECURS after each contract and after any purchase. The daemon now AUTO-HEALS it — sp-os5s (deployed) auto-refreshes on 4204 / cargo-mismatch / foreign-cargo / post-purchase signatures — so treat this as a recurrence-watch, not a manual routine. Symptoms: (a) a deterministic server cargo=0 failure — don't retry; (b) corrupted POSITION → API 4204 "already at destination"; (c) a phantom FOREIGN-CARGO hold silently BENCHES a hauler from the coordinator's eligible pool (looks half-full → not claimable); (d) a freshly-purchased ship caches EMPTY Role → invisible to role-based coordinators; (e) a hauler idle across sessions with stale partial cargo is a refresh candidate, not a lost ship (s81). Manual fallback if auto-heal regresses: `ship refresh --ship <sym>` (force GET /my/ships, allowlisted s32) is the in-band fix — do NOT restart a healthy daemon to clear a phantom. Desyncs strike FLEET-WIDE, so on any negotiate/workflow error naming ship state, refresh the WHOLE POOL (the daemon's dock-retry trusts the stale cache and can never self-heal on its own). If `ship refresh` is unavailable, recover POSITION by navigating to a THIRD waypoint (not the stale one, not the phantom "already-at" one). Role-visibility caveat: a phantom-cleared EXISTING-role idle hauler rejoins the eligible pool IN-LOOP with NO container restart (the contract coordinator re-discovers idle light haulers every contract-accept; s82/d-87 ran 3 contracts post-refresh), but a NEW/empty-role ship is picked up only on the coordinator's next CONTAINER RESTART. Diagnostic: a healthy daemon + RUNNING coordinator + FROZEN ledger emitting zero events was a silent error-loop — now also surfaced as a crash-LOOP detector signal (sp-no9i); check container logs. A merge self-deploy is itself a daemon event: the restart self-heals via ReleaseAllActive (open-era-scoped, sp-s7b7) and accepted contracts resume on next claim, but it reshuffles which hauler serves which stream (~5-10min) — after a deploy, verify every mid-workflow ship still has an executor.
